actions.subject: Array
actions.raw: Array (24 points, plus 3 points of Alternate Effects), Easily Removable (-10 points) • 17 points (this cost includes the cost of the Alternate Effects)

actions.subject: Ability
actions.raw: With it, you can experience adventure as a hero fighting against the forces of evil!

actions.subject: Character
actions.raw: You’ll also help the players develop their own heroes, making sure they fit into the world and have potential for exciting stories in their own right.

actions.subject: Ability
actions.raw: ABILITIES All characters in MUTANTS & MASTERMINDS, from heroes and villains to the average person on the street, are defined by eight abilities, basic traits each character has to a greater or lesser extent.

actions.subject: Character
actions.raw: Characters are said to have training in a skill if they have a rank in that skill.

actions.subject: Character
actions.raw: A character not trained in a skill has no rank;

actions.subject: Ability
actions.raw: Trained characters have a skill rank that adds to the basic ability when making checks.

actions.subject: Ability
actions.raw: If you have the Superhuman or Vessel set of abilities, role once on this table.

actions.subject: Ability
actions.raw: (Choose One) 6, Perception 4, Technology 6 POWERS OFFENSIVE POWERS DODGE +4 PARRY +4 FORTITUDE +4 TOUGHNESS +0 SUPERHUMAN/VESSEL DODGE +4 PARRY +0 FORTITUDE +2 TOUGHNESS +0 WILL +6 WILL +6 If you have the Man of Action Abilities, don’t roll for your Offensive Power, instead, take Find Weakness:

actions.subject: Ability
actions.raw: Power-lifting 2 • 8 points *The first number is if you have the Man of Action set of Abilities and the second number if you have the Superhu- man or Vessel set of Abilities.

actions.subject: Character
actions.raw: Alien, robotic, or artificial life form Shapeshifters often become heroes to win acceptance from the people of their new world.

actions.subject: Ability
actions.raw: THE BASICS 99 MUTANTS & MASTERMINDS MUTANTS & MASTERMINDS DELUXE HERO’S HANDBOOK DELUXE HERO’S HANDBOOK a hero has Agility 6 and is trained in Acrobatics (with a rank of 7) then the character’s bonus for checks involving feats of agility covered by Acrobatics is 13 (6 plus 7).

actions.subject: Advantage
actions.raw: ADVANTAGES Halfway between skills and powers, advantages are minor benefits characters have, allowing them to do things oth- ers cannot.

actions.subject: Advantage
actions.raw: Many advantages have no rank, or rather just one rank;

actions.subject: Advantage
actions.raw: a character either has the advantage (and the benefits that it grants) or does not.

actions.subject: Ability
actions.raw: Other advantages may have multiple ranks, like abilities and skills, measuring their effectiveness.

actions.subject: Ability
actions.raw: Like abilities, measures can have negative ranks.

actions.subject: Effect
actions.raw: A power may have just one effect or several, and you can apply various modifiers to the effects to change how they work, customizing them to get just the right power.

actions.subject: Ability
actions.raw: Power effects have ranks like abilities do, on a scale from 1 to 20 (sometimes more).

actions.subject: Ability
actions.raw: Unlike abilities, effects do not have ranks of less than 1, since the “average” is not having powers at all!

actions.subject: Check
actions.raw: Some power effects require checks to use, while others operate automatically.

actions.subject: Effect
actions.raw: For full descriptions of the various effects powers can have, see the Powers chapter.

actions.subject: Character
actions.raw: COMPLICATIONS Finally, heroes often have complications to overcome.

actions.subject: Character
actions.raw: Overcoming such challenges is part of what makes a real hero.

actions.subject: Ability
actions.raw: So you might say a hero has “rank 8 Strength” or simply “Strength 8” (which mean the same thing), or that an effect is rank 5, 9, 15, or what have you.

actions.subject: Advantage
actions.raw: Then roll on the Advantages II table as directed.

actions.subject: Character
actions.raw: (Choose One) 6 Take the skills listed above, then choose between Set I or Set II below (you may wait to make this choice until after you know what weapon your character uses), then roll on the Background, Mental, and Physical Skills tables as directed.

actions.subject: Effect
actions.raw: Burst Area Ranged Damage 5, Unreliable (five uses) • 1 point • Flare Arrow:

actions.subject: Effect
actions.raw: Ranged Penetrating Damage 5, Unreliable (five uses) • 1 point 102102

actions.subject: Power
actions.raw: A Weather Controller’s power may fluctu- ate according to her mood, which may lead to unpre- dictable storms.

actions.subject: Power
actions.raw: A Weather Controller’s powers are difficult to hide, often resulting in a public identity.

actions.subject: Character
actions.raw: Since 10-9=1, the hero can then toss the truck rank 1 distance (60 feet)!

actions.subject: Ability
actions.raw: When the action starts hap- pening, such as when the heroes are staving off a disaster or fighting villains, time becomes more crucial and is broken down into action rounds, and the players generally have to make die rolls to see how their heroes do.

actions.subject: Character
actions.raw: Whenever a character in MUTANTS & MASTERMINDS attempts something where the outcome is in doubt, it requires a check of an appropriate trait:

actions.subject: Check
actions.raw: Make a check by rolling the die, adding the appropriate rank, and comparing the result against a difficulty class (DC):

actions.subject: Check
actions.raw: smashing through the skylight ...” Breaking some- thing can require a check, but in this case—a body hurling through Plexiglas—the Gamemaster does not see a point in making one, so this is automatic, too.

actions.subject: Character
actions.raw: If your hero has the Swing- ing movement effect (see Powers), then this is auto- matic.

actions.subject: Character
actions.raw: even then, if your hero is good enough at Acrobatics, the Gamemaster might call this a routine check and waive the need for a roll (see Routine Checks later in this chapter).

actions.subject: Ability
actions.raw: If you decided to have your hero shoot or throw something at the villain that would be a check of Dexterity instead.

actions.subject: Ability
actions.raw: So it all depends on the outcome of the Fighting check against the bad guy.

actions.subject: Ability
actions.raw: ABILITIES Everyone has certain basic abilities:

actions.subject: Character
actions.raw: Stronger characters can lift greater weights, more agile characters have better balance, tougher characters can soak up more damage, and so forth.

actions.subject: Ability
actions.raw: MUTANTS & MASTERMINDS characters have eight basic abilities:

actions.subject: Ability
actions.raw: ABILITY RANKS Each ability has a rank associated with it, based on how above or below average it is.

actions.subject: Ability
actions.raw: Sometimes your rank is used to calculate another value, such as when you use your Agility to determine how good you are at avoiding harm with your reflexes (your Dodge defense).

actions.subject: Ability
actions.raw: peak of human achievement Low superhuman Moderate superhuman High superhuman Very high superhuman Cosmic Although a rank of 7 is defined as “the peak of human achievement” in an ability on the Ability Benchmarks table, a character with an ability rank greater than 7 isn’t necessarily “non-human,” merely superhuman in comparison to ordinary people.

actions.subject: Ability
actions.raw: Many “normal human” characters in the comics have truly superhuman abilities, particularly mental abilities.

actions.subject: Ability
actions.raw: A character can have a superhuman ability rank without necessarily being anything other than an amazingly talented, well-trained human being.

actions.subject: Ability
actions.raw: On the other hand, a not so bright (low Intellect) character may have great deal of common sense (high Awareness).

actions.subject: Ability
actions.raw: The key differences between Enhanced Abilities and normal ability ranks are Enhanced Abilities can be nullified (normal abilities cannot, see Nullify, page 173) and Enhanced Abilities can have power modifiers and be used for power stunts with extra effort (normal abilities cannot, see Extra Effort, page 19).

actions.subject: Ability
actions.raw: Enhanced Abilities and normal abilities have the same cost (2 power points per +1 ability rank).

actions.subject: Ability
actions.raw: Whenever an ability rank changes, all traits associated with the ability change as well.

actions.subject: Ability
actions.raw: Debilitated Intellect, Awareness, or Presence means the hero is unaware and remains so until re- stored to at least a –5 rank in the ability.

actions.subject: Ability
actions.raw: A creature with no Strength is incapable of exerting any physical force, either because it has no physical form (like an incorporeal ghost) or simply can’t move (like a tree).

actions.subject: Ability
actions.raw: A creature with no Stamina has no physical body (like a ghost) or is not a living being (such as a robot or other construct).

actions.subject: Ability
actions.raw: A creature with no Agility is unable to move its body under its own power and has no Dodge de- fense.

actions.subject: Ability
actions.raw: MUTANTS & MASTERMINDS heroes cannot be absent an ability without Gamemaster permission, as it can have significant effects on the character and the game.

actions.subject: Ability
actions.raw: A creature with no Fighting is incapable of making any sort of close attack (but may still be able to launch ranged attacks, if it has Dexterity).

actions.subject: Ability
actions.raw: Anything with no Intellect is immune to mental effects and in- teraction skills and has no Will defense.

actions.subject: Ability
actions.raw: Anything with no Awareness is com- pletely unaware and also has no Presence.

actions.subject: Ability
actions.raw: Objects are immune to mental effects and interaction skills, and have no Inanimate objects have no abilities other than their Toughness.

actions.subject: Ability
actions.raw: Animate, but nonliving, constructs such as ro- bots or zombies have Strength, Agility, and Dexterity, and may have ranks of Awareness and Presence (if aware of their environment or capable of interaction), and Fighting (if able to make close attacks).

actions.subject: Ability
actions.raw: They may have Intellect (if capable of independent thought), but have no Stamina (since they are not living things).

actions.subject: Ability
actions.raw: A hero’s defenses are abilities used to avoid such things, determining the difficulty to affect a hero with an attack, or to make resistance checks against them.

actions.subject: Defense
actions.raw: For more on defenses in general and how you use them, see Chapter 8.

actions.subject: Ability
actions.raw: Your base defense ranks are equal to your ranks in their associated abilities.

actions.subject: Defense
actions.raw: ACTIVE DEFENSES Dodge and Parry defenses require a measure of action to be fully effective.

actions.subject: Defense
actions.raw: DEFENSE CLASS One use of defenses is determining a defense class, or the difficulty class to affect a target with a particular at- tack.

actions.subject: Attack
actions.raw: So hitting a character with a ranged attack goes against Dodge defense, giving the attack a DC of (Dodge + 10).

actions.subject: Defense
actions.raw: Similarly, affecting someone with a mental power goes against Will defense, with a DC of (Will + 10), and so forth.

actions.subject: Ability
actions.raw: RESISTANCE CHECKS Defenses are also used to measure the ability to over- come certain effects, involving a resistance check of the defense plus a die roll against a difficulty class determined by the effect or hazard.

actions.subject: Character
actions.raw: INITIATIVE When things start happening quickly, MUTANTS & MASTER- MINDS characters use their initiative bonuses to determine who goes first.

actions.subject: Ability
actions.raw: Initiative Modifier = Agility + Advantages (Improved Initiative) + Power Modifiers Characters then act in initiative order, from highest to low- est.

actions.subject: Attack
actions.raw: Whether or not the hero is successful in stopping the trap depends on the outcome of the attack against the bad guy.

actions.subject: Check
actions.raw: For detailed examples of how to use checks in the game and their effects, see the Action & Adventure chapter.

actions.subject: Check
actions.raw: DIFFICULTY CLASS Checks are made against a difficulty class or DC, a num- ber set by the GM, which your check must equal or exceed to achieve success.

actions.subject: Character
actions.raw: ROUTINE CHECKS DIFFICULTY CLASSES DIFFICULTY (DC) EXAMPLE (SKILL USED) Very easy (0) Notice something in plain sight (Perception) Easy (5) Climb a knotted rope (Athletics) Average (10) Tough (15) Challenging (20) Formidable (25) Heroic (30) Super-heroic (35) Nigh-impossible (40) Hear an approaching security guard (Perception) Disarm an explosive (Technology) Swim against a strong current (Athletics) Climb a wet, slippery rock-face (Athletics) Overcome a sophisticated security system (Technology) Convinces the guards, even though you’re not wearing an ID badge and aren’t on their list, they should let you into the building (Deception) Track a trained commando through the jungle on a moonless night after 12 days of rainfall (Perception) CHECK EXAMPLES TASK CHECK USING...

actions.subject: Character
actions.raw: If a character’s routine check result is not up to a task, the player still has the option to roll the die, since the task is by definition not routine for that character.

actions.subject: Skill
actions.raw: In MUTANTS & MASTERMINDS, they do so through the use of various skills, described in this chapter.

actions.subject: Skill
actions.raw: Each skill has a rank, used as a bonus to the die roll when using the skill.

actions.subject: Ability
actions.raw: d20 + skill rank + ability modifier + miscellaneous modifiers SKILL RANK CRITICAL SUCCESS Your rank in a skill, based on the points you have invested in that skill.

actions.subject: Ability
actions.raw: Each skill has an ability modifier applied to the skill’s checks.

actions.subject: Ability
actions.raw: If you use a skill untrained, the ability modifier still applies to the skill check.

actions.subject: Advantage
actions.raw: MISCELLANEOUS MODIFIERS Miscellaneous modifiers to skill checks include modifiers for circumstances, and bonuses from advantages or pow- ers, among others.

actions.subject: Advantage
actions.raw: Those with the right combinations of skills and advantages can even hold their own against super-powered opponents.

actions.subject: Check
actions.raw: When you use a skill, make a skill check to see how you do.

actions.subject: Skill
actions.raw: Based on the circumstances, your result must match or beat a particular number to use the skill successfully.

actions.subject: Check
actions.raw: (See Checks, page 12, for more information.) UNTRAINED SKILL CHECKS Generally, if you attempt a task requiring a skill you don’t have, you make a skill check as normal.

actions.subject: Skill
actions.raw: Attempts to use these skills untrained automatically fail.

actions.subject: Skill
actions.raw: Since interaction skills are intended for dealing with others so- cially, they have certain requirements.

actions.subject: Check
actions.raw: If they can’t hear or understand you for some reason, you have a –5 circumstance penalty to your skill check (see Circumstance Modifiers in The Basics).

actions.subject: Skill
actions.raw: So, you’ll find climbing isn’t its own skill, but is listed as part of Athletics, while bluffing and search are under Deception and Investiga- tion, respectively.

actions.subject: Effect
actions.raw: The GM decides if a particular use of an interaction skill is effective against a group, and may apply modifiers depending on the situation.

actions.subject: Check
actions.raw: everyone in the group must be able to hear and understand you, for example, or you suffer a –5 on your skill check against them.

actions.subject: Skill
actions.raw: MANIPULATION SKILLS Some skills, called manipulation skills, require a degree of fine physical manipulation.

actions.subject: Ability
actions.raw: If your physical ma- nipulation capabilities are impaired in some fashion (such as having your hands tied or full use of only one hand), the GM may impose a circumstance modifier based on the severity of the impairment.

actions.subject: Ability
actions.raw: Characters lacking the ability to use manipulation skills can still have ranks in them and use them to oversee or assist the work of others (see Team Checks, page 16).

actions.subject: Character
actions.raw: This section describes the skills available to MUTANTS & MASTERMINDS characters, including their common uses and modi- fiers.

actions.subject: Character
actions.raw: Characters may be able to use skills for tasks other than those given here.

actions.subject: DifficultyClass
actions.raw: The GM sets the DC and decides the results in those cases.

actions.subject: Ability
actions.raw: SKILL NAME Ability • Trained Only • Interaction • Manipulation • Requires Tools The skill name line and the line below it contain the fol- lowing information:

actions.subject: Skill
actions.raw: If “Trained Only” is included on the line be- low the skill’s name, you must have at least 1 rank in the skill in order to use it.

actions.subject: Character
actions.raw: If “Trained Only” is absent, untrained characters (those with 0 ranks in the skill) may use it.

actions.subject: Skill
actions.raw: Some skills may have trained only aspects, in which case this notation is still listed, and the untrained aspects are called out in the skill description.

actions.subject: Skill
actions.raw: If “Requires Tools” is included on the line below the skill’s name, you need to have the proper tools to use the skill.

actions.subject: Ability
actions.raw: ACROBATICS Agility • Trained Only Use Acrobatics to flip, dive, roll, tumble, and perform other acrobatic maneuvers, as well as keeping your balance un- der difficult circumstances.

actions.subject: Check
actions.raw: BALANCING You can keep your balance and move along a precari- ous surface at your ground speed minus 1 rank with a successful Acrobatics check against the surface’s DC.

actions.subject: Condition
actions.raw: BALANCING DIFFICULTIES EXAMPLE SURFACE A yard or more wide Wide ledge (1-3 ft.) Narrow ledge (less than 1 ft.) Balance beam Tightrope CIRCUMSTANCE MODIFIERS Surface slightly slippery Surface very slippery Surface slightly uneven Surface very uneven or angled Move at your normal speed rank Not vulnerable while balancing DC 0 5 10 15 20 +2 +5 +2 +5 +5 +5 You are vulnerable while balancing.

actions.subject: Check
actions.raw: If you fail a resistance check while balancing, make an- other immediate Acrobatics check against the original DC to avoid falling.

actions.subject: Ability
actions.raw: ACROBATICS DIFFICULTIES DC 5 15 20 30 TASK Lessen damage from a fall (–1 per degree) Acrobatic maneuver Move from prone to standing as a free action Contort to fit through a tight space ATHLETICS Strength Use Athletics for physical feats like climbing, jumping, rid- ing animal mounts, and swimming.

actions.subject: Check
actions.raw: letics check to catch yourself (DC equal to the initial check + 10).

actions.subject: Attack
actions.raw: CLIMBING DIFFICULTIES EXAMPLE SURFACE A ladder A knotted rope A rope An uneven surface, like a rock-face A rough surface, like a brick wall CIRCUMSTANCE MODIFIERS An air duct, chimney, or other area where you can brace against two opposite walls A corner where you can brace against perpendicular walls Climb of less than 10 feet total Surface slightly slippery Surface very slippery +1 speed rank (up to your full speed) Not vulnerable while climbing DC 0 5 10 15 20 –10 –5 –5 +2 +5 +5 +5 Since you can’t easily move to avoid attacks, you are vul- nerable while climbing unless you accept a +5 increase in the DC.

actions.subject: Check
actions.raw: Any time you fail a resistance check while climb- ing, make an immediate Athletics check against the DC of the climb.

actions.subject: Ability
actions.raw: SWIMMING DC +5 +5 +5 MODIFIERS Rescuing another character who cannot swim Rough or choppy water +1 speed rank (up to your full ground speed) +10 Stormy or turbulent water CLOSE COMBAT Fighting You’re trained with a particular type of close attack, giv- ing you a bonus to your attack checks with it equal to your skill rank (see Attack Check in The Basics and in the Action & Adventure chapter).

actions.subject: Ability
actions.raw: For a broader bonus to attack checks that is less than simply raising Fighting rank, see the Close Attack advantage in the Advantages chapter.

actions.subject: Check
actions.raw: Uses of Deception in action rounds are generally standard actions, although you can attempt to deceive as a move action by taking a –5 penalty to your check.

actions.subject: Check
actions.raw: For instance, if the tar- get gets a +10 bonus to resistance because the deception demands serious risk, and the resistance check succeeds by 10 or less, then the target doesn’t so much see through the deception as prove reluctant to go along with it.

actions.subject: Check
actions.raw: If the target’s Insight check succeeds by 11 or more, he has seen through the deception, and would have refused even if it had not placed unusual demands on him (that is, even without the +10 modifier).

actions.subject: Character
actions.raw: TALENT In game terms there’s no difference between a character who has ranks in a skill because of extensive training and an- other whose skill ranks represent a natural talent or aptitude for the skill.

actions.subject: Character
actions.raw: For example, one character might have a high Persuasion skill based on the character’s extensive training in negotiation, debate, and management.

actions.subject: Character
actions.raw: Another character’s Persuasion skill may stem more from personal attractiveness or a knack for getting others to cooperate, while a third character may have a combination of the two.

actions.subject: Character
actions.raw: LIFE SKILLS When allocating skill ranks for your character consider not just the character’s role as a hero but also the various other skills the character may have picked up in day-to-day life.

actions.subject: Skill
actions.raw: For example, most adults have some sort of Expertise skill as their occupation with at least 3 to 5 ranks (more if they’re especially good at their job).

actions.subject: Character
actions.raw: Characters working with technology may have the Technology skill even if it doesn’t apply to their powers.

actions.subject: Skill
actions.raw: A particularly well-educated person may have various Expertise skills for jobs they don’t even hold.

actions.subject: Character
actions.raw: Some are obvious, especially if they’re part of your character concept.

actions.subject: Equipment
actions.raw: A pilot should have Vehicles, while a doctor should have Treatment in addition to Expertise:

actions.subject: Skill
actions.raw: SKILLS MUTANTS & MASTERMINDS MUTANTS & MASTERMINDS DISGUISE You can use makeup, costumes, and other props to change your appearance.

actions.subject: Check
actions.raw: Successfully acting like who you appear to be may also require a Deception check with a DC equal to the observ- er’s Insight check, modified by familiarity if the observer knows the subject well, as mentioned previously.

actions.subject: Attack
actions.raw: If your Deception check succeeds, the tar- get is vulnerable against your next attack, until the end of your next round (see Vulnerable in the Conditions sec- tion of The Basics chapter).

actions.subject: DifficultyClass
actions.raw: Com- plex messages or messages trying to communicate new information have DCs of 15 or 20, respectively.

actions.subject: Check
actions.raw: The recipi- ent of the message must make a Insight check against the same DC to understand it.

actions.subject: Check
actions.raw: INNUENDO DIFFICULTIES DC 10 15 20 TASK Basic message Complex message Message containing new or detailed information Anyone listening in on your innuendo can attempt a In- sight check against the message DC.

actions.subject: Attack
actions.raw: If your Deception check opposed by Decep- tion or Insight succeeds, your opponent is heedless of the potential danger and may hit the junction box or lose his balance and fall, if his attack against you fails.

actions.subject: Skill
actions.raw: Each is considered a separate skill and training in each is acquired separately, so a former police officer turned district attorney might have Expertise:

actions.subject: Check
actions.raw: The GM sets DCs for specific tasks using the guidelines provided in The Basics chapter under Checks, keeping in mind that most job-related checks should be considered routine (see Routine Checks in that section).

actions.subject: Skill
actions.raw: INNUENDO You can use Insight to pick up on hidden messages sent via the Deception skill (see the Deception skill descrip- tion).

actions.subject: Ability
actions.raw: INTIMIDATION Presence • Interaction You know how to use threats (both real and implied) to get others to do what you want.

actions.subject: Check
actions.raw: COERCING Make an Intimidation check, opposed by the target’s In- sight or Will defense (whichever has the highest bonus).

actions.subject: Check
actions.raw: Uses of Intimidation in ac- tion rounds are generally standard actions, although you can attempt to deceive as a move action by taking a –5 penalty to your check.

actions.subject: Character
actions.raw: SKILLS 121121 MUTANTS & MASTERMINDS MUTANTS & MASTERMINDS DELUXE HERO’S HANDBOOK DELUXE HERO’S HANDBOOK ceed or fail, a target’s true attitude towards you generally becomes hostile after you attempt an Intimidation check, even if they go along with you for the moment.

actions.subject: Advantage
actions.raw: If the group has you at a disadvantage, you suffer the usual circumstance penalty on your check.

actions.subject: Check
actions.raw: Compare your check result against a single resistance check made by the GM for the entire group.

actions.subject: Check
actions.raw: Your Intimidation check must have the same effect on every member of the group (that is, you cannot demor- alize some and coerce others, for example).

actions.subject: Check
actions.raw: Rocky has no interest in hurting a bunch of kids, so he bellows, “Get outta the way or I’ll knock your blocks off!” and his player decides to use Rocky’s routine Intimi- dation check of 18 to attempt to coerce the entire group of minions into moving out of his way.

actions.subject: Defense
actions.raw: The street kids are all Thugs, so they have a resistance rank of 0 (their Insight and Will ranks are tied).

actions.subject: Check
actions.raw: The GM may make In- vestigation checks for you in secret, so you do not know exactly what you have found, or if you may have missed something.

actions.subject: Check
actions.raw: By succeeding at a DC 10 Investigation check taking at least an hour, you get a feel for the major news and ru- mors in an area.

actions.subject: Check
actions.raw: The DC of the subject’s Stealth check to evade your notice is equal to the result of your Investigation check.

actions.subject: Ability
actions.raw: PERCEPTION Awareness MODIFIERS Every day since event (max modifier +10) Crime scene is outdoors Crime scene disturbed Crime scene highly disturbed Use this skill to notice and pick up on things.

actions.subject: Check
actions.raw: Discerning details—such as clearly hearing conversation or reading fine text—requires at least three degrees of success on the Perception check.

actions.subject: Check
actions.raw: In general, you have a –1 circumstance penalty to Percep- tion checks for every 10 feet between you and what you are +2 +5 +2 +5

actions.subject: Check
actions.raw: HEARING Make a check against a DC based on how loud the noise is or against an opposed Stealth check.

actions.subject: Check
actions.raw: SEEING Make a check against a DC based on how visible the ob- ject is or against an opposed Stealth check.

actions.subject: DifficultyClass
actions.raw: Less obvious things are DC 10 or so, hidden things DC 20 or more, and discerning details requires at least three de- grees of success, as usual.

actions.subject: Character
actions.raw: Non-player characters each have an initial attitude to- wards you or your cause.

actions.subject: Defense
actions.raw: Unfavorable Will insult, mislead, or otherwise cause you trouble.

actions.subject: Attack
actions.raw: The heroes must convince the imperi- ous King of Atlantis that the surface world is not responsible for recent attacks against his king- dom in order to avert a war.

actions.subject: Attack
actions.raw: The he- roes try to convince the king further, but any ad- ditional checks need at least the same degree of success as the first to get his attitude to favorable, where he is willing to call off the attack, and more than one degree of failure on any check moves his attitude to hostile, where he orders the intruders arrested and the attack to begin at once!

actions.subject: Ability
actions.raw: RANGED COMBAT Dexterity You’re trained with a particular type of ranged attack, giving you a bonus to your attack checks with it equal to your skill rank (see Attack Check in The Basics and in the Action & Adventure chapter).

actions.subject: Ability
actions.raw: For a broader bonus to attack checks that is less than simply raising Dexterity rank, see the Ranged Attack advantage in the Advantages chapter.

actions.subject: Skill
actions.raw: Stage magicians use Sleight of Hand legitimately as a performance skill, but it is most commonly known for its criminal applications.

actions.subject: Check
actions.raw: CONCEALING You can use Sleight of Hand to conceal a small item on your person, making your check result the DC of an Inves- tigation or Perception check to find it.

actions.subject: Check
actions.raw: USING OPPOSED CHECKS TASK SKILL OPPOSED BY Sneak up on someone Stealth Perception Con someone Deception Win a car race Vehicles Insight Vehicles Pretend to be someone else Steal a key chain unnoticed Deception Perception Sleight of Hand Perception Win a trivia contest Expertise Expertise Break computer security DEGREES OF SUCCESS AND FAILURE Technology Technology CHECK RESULT EQUAL OR GREATER THAN DEGREE EQUAL OR GREATER THAN ...

actions.subject: Character
actions.raw: They are made against an- other character’s check result as the DC.

actions.subject: Attack
actions.raw: Active defenses in combat, where characters are focusing on other actions, are generally routine opposition, which is why attack checks are made against a DC of 10 + the ap- propriate defense.

actions.subject: Ability
actions.raw: COMPARISON CHECKS In cases where a check is a simple test of one character’s capability against another, with no luck involved, both participants compare their appropriate ranks.

actions.subject: Character
actions.raw: Note this does not in- clude the use of extra effort (see page 19) to temporarily increase a character’s rank, which can affect the outcome of a comparison check, nor does it include things like ma- neuvers, tricks, or other ways of trying to affect the out- come.

actions.subject: DifficultyClass
actions.raw: LEGERDEMAIN Minor feats of sleight of hand, such as making a coin or playing card “vanish,” have a DC of 10 unless an observer is focused on noticing what you are doing.

actions.subject: Skill
actions.raw: He has Skill Mastery for his Stealth and, unsurprisingly, the guard doesn’t notice him slither closer, but then the Rubber-Bandit decides to try and steal the guard’s gun without being noticed.

actions.subject: Skill
actions.raw: The guard, with a Perception skill of only +5, doesn’t have a prayer of noticing his gun being eased out of its holster, but the GM rolls anyway and gets a total of 20.

actions.subject: Character
actions.raw: SKILLS MUTANTS & MASTERMINDS MUTANTS & MASTERMINDS HIDING DELUXE HERO’S HANDBOOK DELUXE HERO’S HANDBOOK OPERATING If you have cover or concealment, make a Stealth check, opposed by an observer’s Perception check, to hide and go unnoticed.

actions.subject: Ability
actions.raw: If others are aware of your presence, you can’t use Stealth to remain undetected.

actions.subject: Check
actions.raw: (As a general guideline, any such cover has to be within 1 foot for every rank you have in Stealth.) This check, however, is at a –5 penalty because you have to move quickly.

actions.subject: Check
actions.raw: TECHNOLOGY Most routine operations of technological equipment don’t require a skill check and can be done untrained.

actions.subject: Check
actions.raw: Us- ing an unfamiliar device does require a check, with the DC determined by how foreign or unusual the device is, from simple (DC 10) to highly advanced (DC 25 or more).

actions.subject: Check
actions.raw: If your Technology check succeeds, you have made the item after the necessary time.

actions.subject: Check
actions.raw: More than one degree of failure on the check may produce an accident or other unfortunate side-effect at the GM’s discretion.

actions.subject: DifficultyClass
actions.raw: REPAIRING You can also use Technology to repair damaged items, with a –5 to the DC to build the item and –2 to the time rank required.

actions.subject: Ability
actions.raw: Intellect • Trained Only • Manipulation • Requires Tools DEMOLITIONS Technology covers operating, building, repairing, and generally working with technological devices and equip- ment.

actions.subject: Ability
actions.raw: Careful placement of an explosive against a fixed struc- ture can maximize damage by exploiting vulnerabilities in the structure.

actions.subject: Check
actions.raw: This requires at least a minute and a DC 10 Technology check.

actions.subject: Check
actions.raw: The GM makes the check, so you don’t know exactly how well you have done until the explosive BUILDING ITEMS DC 15 20 25 30 COMPLEXITY TIME RANK EXAMPLES Simple Moderate Complex Advanced 10 (2 hours) electronic timer or detonator, tripwire trap 12 (8 hours) radio direction finder, lock, engine component 14 (24 hours) cell phone, combustion engine 16 (4 days) computer, vehicle

actions.subject: Check
actions.raw: Disarming an explosive also requires a Technology check.

actions.subject: Advantage
actions.raw: INVENTING If you have the Inventor advantage (see the Advantages chapter), you can use Technology to create inventions, temporary devices.

actions.subject: Device
actions.raw: SECURITY You can use Technology to disarm or sabotage various security devices, including locks, traps, and sensors.

actions.subject: Check
actions.raw: The GM makes your Technology check secretly so you don’t necessarily know right away if you have suc- ceeded.

actions.subject: Ability
actions.raw: Intellect • Trained Only • Manipulation • Requires Tools STABILIZE You’re trained in treating injuries and ailments.

actions.subject: Check
actions.raw: If you do not have the appropriate medical equipment and supplies, you take a –5 circumstance penalty on your check.

actions.subject: Condition
actions.raw: Other conditions the patient may have remain, so reviving someone inca- pacitated due to fatigue still leaves the patient exhaust- ed, for example, while awakening someone incapacitat- ed due to damage still leaves the patient staggered.

actions.subject: Character
actions.raw: Each time the character makes a resistance check against the ailment, you make a Treatment check.

actions.subject: Ability
actions.raw: VEHICLES Dexterity • Trained Only • Manipulation Use this skill to operate vehicles, from ground vehicles like cars to boats, planes, or even spaceships!

actions.subject: Check
actions.raw: Routine tasks, such as ordinary operation of known vehi- cles, don’t require a check and may even be done untrained for some vehicles, particularly common ones like cars.

actions.subject: Ability
actions.raw: ADVANTAGES Heroes are more than just skilled, they often have amazing advantages, beyond the abilities of ordinary people.

actions.subject: Advantage
actions.raw: Some advantages have no ranks and are acquired only once, effectively at rank 1.

actions.subject: Advantage
actions.raw: Ranks in a advantage are noted with a number after the advantage’s name, such as “Defensive Roll 2” (for a character who has taken two ranks in the Defensive Roll advantage), just like skill and power ranks.

actions.subject: Advantage
actions.raw: Fortune Advantages require and enhance the use of hero points.

actions.subject: Advantage
actions.raw: Skill Advantages offer bonuses or modifications to skill use.

actions.subject: Advantage
actions.raw: In some cases a advantage’s description mentions the normal conditions for characters who do not have the advantage for comparison.

actions.subject: Skill
actions.raw: AGILE FEINT SKILL You can use your Acrobatics bonus or movement speed rank in place of Deception to feint and trick in combat as if your skill bonus or speed rank were your Deception bonus (see the Deception skill description).

actions.subject: Ability
actions.raw: Characters normally have a –10 circumstance penalty to use interac- tion skills on animals, due to their Intellect and lack of lan- guage.

actions.subject: Skill
actions.raw: ARTIFICER SKILL You can use the Expertise:

actions.subject: Check
actions.raw: CIRCUMSTANCES AND DIFFICULTY There is no limit to the number of degrees a check may have, although more than two degrees of failure rarely matters, and some degrees of success may have no further effect be- yond a certain point (once you have succeeded as well as is possible in a given situation).

actions.subject: Advantage
actions.raw: Characters in a fa- vorable situation are said to have a circumstance bonus for the check, while those in a disadvantageous situation are said to be have a circumstance penalty.

actions.subject: Effect
actions.raw: Improvised Weapon Use Unarmed Combat skill with improvised weapons, +1 damage bonus.

actions.subject: Attack
actions.raw: Use Deception to redirect a missed attack at another target.

actions.subject: Check
actions.raw: Choose a target you can accurately perceive and have the GM make a secret Insight check for you as a free action, opposed by the target’s Deception check result.

actions.subject: Power
actions.raw: This bonus does not count as part of your regular skill bonus in terms of the series power level, but also does not apply to people or situations which (in the GM’s opinion) would not be in- fluenced by your appearance.

actions.subject: Ability
actions.raw: GENERAL ADVANTAGES EFFECT Use Insight to learn an opponent’s combat capabilities.

actions.subject: Skill
actions.raw: Use interaction skills normally with animals.

actions.subject: Skill
actions.raw: Use an interaction skill to entrance others.

actions.subject: Check
actions.raw: Circumstance bonus to checks against a type of opponent.

actions.subject: Device
actions.raw: Use Technology to create temporary devices.

actions.subject: Check
actions.raw: Take a standard action and make an interaction skill check against your target’s opposing check (Insight or Will defense).

actions.subject: Skill
actions.raw: Like all interaction skills, you can use Fascinate on a group, but you must affect everyone in the group in the same way.

actions.subject: Attack
actions.raw: FAST GRAB COMBAT When you hit with an unarmed attack you can immedi- ately make a grab check against that opponent as a free action (see Grab, page 248).

actions.subject: Effect
actions.raw: FAVORED FOE SKILL You have a particular type of opponent you’ve studied or are especially effective against.

actions.subject: Check
actions.raw: GREAT ENDURANCE GENERAL You have a +5 bonus on checks to avoid becoming fa- tigued and checks to hold your breath, avoid damage from starvation or thirst, avoid damage from hot or cold environments, and to resist suffocation and drowning.

actions.subject: Attack
actions.raw: IMPROVED DISARM COMBAT You have no penalty to your attack check when attempt- ing to disarm an opponent and they do not get the op- portunity to disarm you (see Disarm in the Action & Ad- venture chapter).

actions.subject: Advantage
actions.raw: IMPROVED INITIATIVE COMBAT, RANKED You have a +4 bonus to your initiative checks per rank in this advantage.

actions.subject: Attack
actions.raw: IMPROVED SMASH COMBAT You have no penalty to attack checks to hit an object held by another character (see Smash in the Action & Adven- ture chapter).

actions.subject: Attack
actions.raw: IMPROVED TRIP COMBAT You have no penalty to your attack check to trip an op- ponent and they do not get the opportunity to trip you.

actions.subject: Attack
actions.raw: When making a trip attack, make an opposed check of your Acrobatics or Athletics against your opponent’s Ac- robatics or Athletics, you choose which your opponent uses to defend, rather than the target choosing (see Trip in the Action & Adventure chapter).

actions.subject: Skill
actions.raw: IMPROVISED WEAPON COMBAT, RANKED When wielding an improvised close combat weapon— anything from a chair to a telephone pole or entire car— you use your Close Combat:

actions.subject: Character
actions.raw: At the GM’s discretion, characters may have to spend an additional Languages rank to be literate in a language with a different alphabet or style of writing from the character’s native language (such as Arabic, Japanese kanji or Russian Cyrillic for an English speaker).

actions.subject: Skill
actions.raw: INVENTOR SKILL You can use the Technology skill to create inventions.

actions.subject: Advantage
actions.raw: ADVANTAGES 139139 MUTANTS & MASTERMINDS MUTANTS & MASTERMINDS DELUXE HERO’S HANDBOOK DELUXE HERO’S HANDBOOK initiative result, followed by all the other characters who do not have this advantage.

actions.subject: Attack
actions.raw: For example, you can feint and have your target vulnerable against one or more allies next attack(s), rather than yours.

actions.subject: Check
actions.raw: The interaction skill check requires its normal action, and the affected allies must be capable of interacting with you (or at least seeing the set-up) to benefit from it.

actions.subject: Power
actions.raw: A sidekick’s power point total must be less than yours.

actions.subject: Character
actions.raw: Gamemasters should generally allow you to control your sidekick, although sidekicks re- main NPCs and the GM has final say in their actions.

actions.subject: Character
actions.raw: Sidekicks also do not have hero points, but you can spend your own hero points on the sidekick’s behalf with the usual bene- fits.

actions.subject: Check
actions.raw: When you support a team check (see Team Checks in The Basics chapter) you have a +5 circumstance bonus to your check.

actions.subject: Advantage
actions.raw: THROWING MASTERY COMBAT, RANKED You have a +1 damage bonus with thrown weapons per rank in this advantage.

actions.subject: Effect
actions.raw: TRACKING SKILL SKILL MASTERY SKILL You can use the Perception skill to visually follow tracks like the Tracking Senses effect (see the Powers chapter).

actions.subject: Skill
actions.raw: STARTLE SKILL You can use Intimidation rather than Deception to feint in combat (see Feint under the Deception skill description).

actions.subject: Attack
actions.raw: TAKEDOWN COMBAT, RANKED (2) If you render a minion incapacitated with an attack, you get an immediate extra attack as a free action against an- other minion within range and adjacent to the previous target’s location.

actions.subject: Ability
actions.raw: While in the trance you add your Awareness rank to your Stamina rank to de- termine how long you can hold your breath and you use the higher of your Fortitude or Will defenses for resistance checks against suffocation (see Suffocation, page 238).

actions.subject: Ability
actions.raw: It requires a Perception check with a DC equal to your Awareness check result to determine you’re not dead because your bodily functions are so slow.

actions.subject: Attack
actions.raw: WEAPON BIND COMBAT If you take the defend action (see Defend in the Action & Adventure chapter) and successfully defend against a close weapon attack, you can make a disarm attempt against the attacker immediately as a reaction.

actions.subject: Attack
actions.raw: WEAPON BREAK COMBAT If you take the defend action (see Defend in the Action & Adventure chapter) and successfully defend against a close weapon attack, you can make an attack against the attacker’s weapon immediately as a reaction.

actions.subject: Character
actions.raw: When encountering an individual, group, or organization for the first time, you can make an immediate Investigation or Persuasion skill check to see if your character has heard something about the subject.

actions.subject: Skill
actions.raw: Use the guidelines for gathering information in the Investigation skill description to determine the lev- el of information you gain.

actions.subject: Character
actions.raw: A hero with the Concealment effect (see page 153) could use it to create a power called Blending, Blur, Cloak, Invisibility, Shadowmeld, or anything else appropriate to the character you wish to play.

actions.subject: Effect
actions.raw: It’s all a matter of how powerful the effect is and what modifiers have been placed on it to increase or decrease its performance.

actions.subject: Effect
actions.raw: The more ranks an effect has, the greater its effect.

actions.subject: Effect
actions.raw: Each effect of a power has a standard cost per rank.

actions.subject: Modifier
actions.raw: Modifiers have ranks, just like other traits.

actions.subject: Character
actions.raw: It’s more inter- esting and clear to say a hero has a “Flame Blast” or “Light- ning Bolt” power than a generic “Damage effect.” “Flame” and “lightning” are descriptors for the Damage effect.

actions.subject: Descriptor
actions.raw: De- scriptors do have some impact on the game since some ef- fects work only on or with effects of a particular descriptor.

actions.subject: Descriptor
actions.raw: Generally speaking, a descriptor is part of what a power is called beyond its game system name.

actions.subject: Character
actions.raw: For example, a weather-controlling heroine has the following effects:

actions.subject: Character
actions.raw: Her Damage effect is the power to throw lightning bolts, so it has the descriptor “lightning.” If a villain can absorb electricity, then his power works against the heroine’s Damage (since lightning is electrical in nature).

actions.subject: Character
actions.raw: Her Environment is the power to control the weather, giving it the descriptor “weather.” If the hero- ine’s power comes as a gift from the gods, it may also have the descriptor “divine” or “magical.” On the other hand, if it comes from her mutant genetic structure, then it has the descriptor “mutant.” A villain able to nullify mutant powers could potentially nullify all of the heroine’s powers!

actions.subject: Character
actions.raw: The players and Gamemaster should cooperate to apply the appropriate descriptors to characters’ powers and use com- mon sense when dealing with how the different descrip- tors interact.

actions.subject: Character
actions.raw: In this case, one character (usually the one with the high- est bonus) is considered the leader of the effort and makes the check normally, while each helper makes the same type of check using the same trait(s) against DC 10.

actions.subject: Character
actions.raw: The GM sets the limit on how many characters can help as part of a team check.

actions.subject: Attack
actions.raw: Attack Check = d20 + attack bonus + modifiers vs.

actions.subject: Check
actions.raw: Resistance Check = d20 + defense bonus + modifiers vs.

actions.subject: Equipment
actions.raw: Move actions also include things like drawing weapons, standing up, and picking up or manipulating objects.

actions.subject: Character
actions.raw: Free actions include things like talking (heroes and villains always find time to say a lot in the middle of a fight), drop- ping something, ending the use of a power, activating or maintaining some other powers, and so forth.

actions.subject: Character
actions.raw: So, for example, the ac- tion of the Flight effect is “free” and activating it grants the character a Flight speed rank equal to the effect rank.

actions.subject: Attack
actions.raw: Others—particularly those affecting other people—require some effort to use, like an attack check or a effect check.

actions.subject: Check
actions.raw: Powers affecting others allow resistance checks against their effects.

actions.subject: DifficultyClass
actions.raw: d20, plus the power’s rank, plus any applicable modifiers, against a difficulty class set by the Gamemaster.

actions.subject: Check
actions.raw: Effect Check = d20 + rank + modifiers vs.

actions.subject: Check
actions.raw: difficulty class ROUTINE EFFECT CHECKS Many power effects allow for routine checks involving their use, generally specified in the effect’s description (see Routine Checks in The Basics chapter).

actions.subject: Check
actions.raw: OPPOSED EFFECT CHECKS In some cases, usually when one effect is used directly against another, or against a particular trait like an abil- ity or skill, an opposed check is called for (see Opposed Checks in The Basics chapter).

actions.subject: Effect
actions.raw: Each effect has certain parameters that describe the time needed to use the effect, the subject or target, the dis- tance it works at, and so forth.

actions.subject: Effect
actions.raw: ACTION Using or activating an effect requires a particular amount of time.

actions.subject: Effect
actions.raw: Modifiers may change the action needed to use an effect.

actions.subject: Effect
actions.raw: Using the effect requires a standard action.

actions.subject: Effect
actions.raw: Using the effect requires a move action.

actions.subject: Attack
actions.raw: or Will 1 per rank 8 per rank 2 per rank 2 per rank 3 per rank 1 per rank 1 per rank 4 per rank 2 per rank 3 per rank 1 per rank 2 per rank 2-5 per rank 7 per rank 1 per rank NAME Senses Shapeshift Shrinking Sleep Snare Speed Strike Suffocation Summon Attack Attack Control Super-Speed See description Movement Movement Control General Attack Swimming Teleport Transform Variable Weaken RANGE Each effect has a default range, which may be changed by modifiers.

actions.subject: Attack
actions.raw: Touching an unwilling subject requires an un- armed attack check against the subject’s Parry.

actions.subject: Attack
actions.raw: The effect works at a distance, limited by perception and path and requiring a ranged attack check against the subject’s Dodge defense.

actions.subject: Character
actions.raw: Willing characters can forgo their resistance check against an effect, if they wish.

actions.subject: Character
actions.raw: This includes characters who think they’re receiving a beneficial effect, even if they’re not!

actions.subject: Character
actions.raw: POWERS 147147 MUTANTS & MASTERMINDS MUTANTS & MASTERMINDS DELUXE HERO’S HANDBOOK DELUXE HERO’S HANDBOOK each other they must have opposed descriptors.

actions.subject: Effect
actions.raw: Effects usable as a reaction do not require a ready action;

actions.subject: Effect
actions.raw: If an opponent attempts to use a power you are able to counter, use your countering effect as your readied ac- tion.

actions.subject: Effect
actions.raw: Having prepared an action, Siren’s player says she wants to counter White Knight’s fire blast with You can also use one power to counter the ongoing effect of another, or other lingering results of an instant effect (like flames ignited by a fiery Damage effect).

actions.subject: Check
actions.raw: This requires a normal use of the countering effect and an opposed check, as above.

actions.subject: Effect
actions.raw: Mastermind has placed Johnny Rocket under his mental control (an Affliction effect).

actions.subject: Effect
actions.raw: Lady Liberty has the power to break such bonds (the Nul- lify effect).

actions.subject: Defense
actions.raw: If she fails, the Freedom League will have to come up with another plan to neutralize their super-fast teammate without hurting him.

actions.subject: Check
actions.raw: AFFLICTION RESISTANCE CHECK Fortitude or Will vs.

actions.subject: Descriptor
actions.raw: Potential descriptors include coughing or sneez- ing, creeping mental influence, drowsiness, euphoria, fear, itchiness, lethargy, nausea, pain, or tipsiness.

actions.subject: Descriptor
actions.raw: Potential descriptors include agonizing pain, confusion, ecstasy, momentary emotional or men- tal influence, paralysis, seizure, terror, or vomiting.

actions.subject: Condition
actions.raw: Third degree conditions require a minute of recovery time or outside aid, such as the Treatment skill or Healing effect (DC 10 + rank).

actions.subject: Effect
actions.raw: So if an effect does not have any particular extras associ- ated with it, the extras entry is omitted.

actions.subject: Attack
actions.raw: Once you have hit with a Concentration Affliction, so long as you continue to take a standard action each turn to maintain the effect, the target must make a new resistance check against it, with no attack check required.

actions.subject: Effect
actions.raw: Normally, an Affliction does not have a cumu- lative effect on the same target, so getting two results of one degree, one after the other, has no more or less effect than a single one degree result;

actions.subject: Effect
actions.raw: If a power has multiple effects, each is listed with its applicable modifiers.

actions.subject: Effect
actions.raw: This is a cost per rank of the power if it has a ranked effect, oth- erwise it is a flat cost in power points.

actions.subject: Power
actions.raw: Some pow- ers may have a flat cost for the initial power, plus a cost per rank for additional ranks.

actions.subject: Condition
actions.raw: For example, if you hit a target and impose a vulnerable condition (one degree), then at- tack again and get one degree on the effect, you impose the Affliction’s second degree condition.

actions.subject: Effect
actions.raw: Since mutually incompatible condi- tions are largely wasted, Afflictions with this extra often have the Limited Degree flaw as well.

actions.subject: Effect
actions.raw: This modifier causes an Affliction to increase incrementally without any effort from you.

actions.subject: Effect
actions.raw: if it requires a move action, subtract 1 power point from the total cost of the effects.

actions.subject: Effect
actions.raw: Some potential Alternate Forms (and their possible effects) include:

actions.subject: Modifier
actions.raw: Essentially, all your powers have the Activa- tion modifier!

actions.subject: Character
actions.raw: The game effect is the same (the hero’s active defenses are reduced), so it is easier to just refer to the overall condition as “vulnerable” and describe the differ- ent situations that cause it.

actions.subject: Condition
actions.raw: Indeed, many power effects reference these basic conditions in the descriptions of what they do.

actions.subject: Character
actions.raw: A controlled character has no free will;

actions.subject: Character
actions.raw: A dazed character is limited to free actions and a single standard action per turn, although the character may use that action to perform a move, as usual.

actions.subject: Ability
actions.raw: The character has one or more abilities lowered below –5.

actions.subject: Character
actions.raw: A defenseless character has active de- fense bonuses of 0.

actions.subject: Attack
actions.raw: Thus, foes with Visual Conceal- ment (the most common accurate sense) can be quite effective against characters relying on perception range attacks, unless the attacker has an unusual accurate sense to circumvent the Concealment.

actions.subject: Check
actions.raw: At the Gamemaster’s discretion, a successful Perception check to accurately locate a target with an acute sense may al- low you to use perception range effects on that target;

actions.subject: Effect
actions.raw: however, the target still benefits from concealment, granting a +5 circumstance bonus to resistance against the effect.

actions.subject: Modifier
actions.raw: Concealment with Affects Others (previously) or At- tack (immediately following) may have this extra, affecting everything in the area.

actions.subject: Effect
actions.raw: Use this extra for a Concealment effect you can impose on others (whether they want to be concealed or not).

actions.subject: Effect
actions.raw: The GM has final say on whether or not a particular object is too complex for this effect.

actions.subject: Descriptor
actions.raw: They can be solid or hollow, opaque or transparent, as you choose when you use the effect, limited by your descriptors and the Gamemaster’s judgment.

actions.subject: Effect
actions.raw: Your created objects are stationary once you have cre- ated them, although other effects can move them.

actions.subject: Attack
actions.raw: This requires both an attack check against the target’s Dodge and a Dodge resistance check against the effect’s rank.

actions.subject: Character
actions.raw: A character able to call up animated snowmen, on the other hand, should have Summon, while a powerful “ice elemental” may very well have both effects!

actions.subject: Condition
actions.raw: Imposing conditions on the target other than just trapping them requires a separate effect, such as Affliction (see Afflic- tion in this chapter), which you may wish to acquire as an Alternate Effect of Create (see Alternate Effect in this chapter).

actions.subject: Check
actions.raw: The object inflicts damage equal to its Toughness, and targets get a Dodge check to evade the falling object.

actions.subject: Ability
actions.raw: SUPPORTING WEIGHT If a created object needs to support weight—created as a bridge or to support a weakened structure, for exam- ple—it has an effective Strength equal to its rank.

actions.subject: Character
actions.raw: POWERS MUTANTS & MASTERMINDS MUTANTS & MASTERMINDS DELUXE HERO’S HANDBOOK DELUXE HERO’S HANDBOOK jects cannot have their selectivity changed once they are created.

actions.subject: Ability
actions.raw: They resist being moved with a Strength rank equal to the modifier rank.

actions.subject: Modifier
actions.raw: Unless you have the Tether ex- tra or the Movable extra, you cannot move a stationary created object once it’s placed any more than anyone else can.

actions.subject: Modifier
actions.raw: This modifier either makes created objects not noticeable as constructs for 1 rank (they look just like real objects) or not noticeable at all for 2 ranks (such as objects composed of invisible force).

actions.subject: Defense
actions.raw: Your created objects have a total volume rank plus Toughness rank equal to your Create rank, rather than both volume and Toughness up to your rank.

actions.subject: Defense
actions.raw: So you can create an object with volume rank 0 and Toughness equal to your Create rank, vice versa, or anywhere in be- tween, so long as the sum of the two ranks does not ex- ceed your Create rank.

actions.subject: Check
actions.raw: The target makes a Forti- tude or Will resistance check against your effect DC (choose one when you acquire the effect).

actions.subject: Check
actions.raw: The target automati- cally fails Perception checks involving the sense, and everything effectively has total concealment from that sense.

actions.subject: Effect
actions.raw: Multiple Dazzle effects against the same sense are cu- mulative.

actions.subject: Check
actions.raw: The target has a –1 circumstance penalty to further resistance checks against damage.

actions.subject: Check
actions.raw: The target is dazed until the end of their next turn and has a –1 circumstance penalty to further checks against damage.

actions.subject: Check
actions.raw: The target is staggered and has a -1 circumstance penalty to further checks against damage.

actions.subject: Character
actions.raw: DELUXE HERO’S HANDBOOK DELUXE HERO’S HANDBOOK The circumstance penalties to Toughness checks are cu- mulative, so a target who fails three resistance checks against Damage, each with one degree of failure, has a total –3 penalty.

actions.subject: Check
actions.raw: If an incapacitated target fails a resistance check against Damage, the target’s condition shifts to dying.

actions.subject: Check
actions.raw: A dying tar- get who fails a resistance check against Damage is dead.

actions.subject: Power
actions.raw: If you have it at a lower rank, create your duplicate as a scaled-down version of yourself, with a power level equal to your rank in this power and starting power points determined accordingly (power rank x 15).

actions.subject: Character
actions.raw: So a power level 11 hero who has Duplication 8 creates a power level 8 “duplicate” with (8 x 15) 120 power points and proportionately lower-ranked traits.

actions.subject: Modifier
actions.raw: Use the Multiple Minions extra to be able to create multiple duplicates.

actions.subject: Condition
actions.raw: Dazed and staggered results have no real effect on inanimate targets, since they do not take actions.

actions.subject: Attack
actions.raw: If an attacker’s intention is to bend, break or destroy an object, then two degrees of failure on the Toughness check results in a bend or break (such as a hole punched through the object) while three or more degrees of fail- ure means the object is destroyed (shattered, smashed to pieces, etc.).

actions.subject: Check
actions.raw: The GM decides the brick, mortar, and heavy beams have a toughness of 9 and makes a Tough- ness check, rolling a 5, against a DC 30 (Lady Lib- erty’s Damage +15).

actions.subject: Defense
actions.raw: Equipment has Toughness based on its material.

actions.subject: Defense
actions.raw: Devices have a base Toughness equal to the total points in the device divided by 5 (rounded down, minimum of 1).

actions.subject: Character
actions.raw: So a damaged character recovers from being inca- pacitated, then staggered, dazed, and finally removes a –1 Toughness check penalty per minute until fully recovered.

actions.subject: Ability
actions.raw: Objects, having no Stamina, do not recover from damage unless they have an effect like Regeneration.

actions.subject: Attack
actions.raw: 1 point per rank You can actively defend for characters other than yourself, deflecting or diverting attacks against them at a distance, and may be able to more effectively defend yourself, de- pending on your rank.

actions.subject: Attack
actions.raw: Like a ranged attack, if you Deflect at medium range, you have a –2 circumstance modifier on your check.

actions.subject: Attack
actions.raw: Like the defend action, Deflect does not work against area effects or perception ranged attacks, nor versus attacks targeting defenses other than Dodge or Parry.

actions.subject: Attack
actions.raw: To create a kind of “deflec- tion field” or similar effect that automatically deflects attacks over a wide area, use an Enhanced Dodge and/or Enhanced Parry effect with modifiers like Area and Selective.

actions.subject: Attack
actions.raw: First, you must successfully deflect the attack, then make a normal attack check using your own attack modifier to hit with the reflected attack.

actions.subject: Power

actions.subject: Modifier

actions.subject: Modifier
actions.raw: The GM may require Extra Limbs with this extra to modify their duration to continuous or sustained, but this is not essen- tial.

actions.subject: Effect
actions.raw: Sustained Extra Limbs can be turned on or off (growing or forming the additional limbs and then mak- ing them disappear just as easily), but the limbs disappear or stop working if you are unable to continue the effect.

actions.subject: Modifier
actions.raw: This flaw should generally not ap- ply to any creature with Innate Extra Limbs, especially if they are part of its natural physiology.

actions.subject: Ability
actions.raw: 1 point per rank You have one or more minor features, effects granting you an occasionally useful ability, one per rank.

actions.subject: Descriptor

actions.subject: Character
actions.raw: There’s no need to define every possible Fea- ture a character may have down to the last detail.

actions.subject: Ability
actions.raw: The Feature effect is intended to round out various minor traits and abilities characters might have, but it is entirely optional and not meant to burden MUTANTS & MASTERMINDS character design with needless amounts of detail.

actions.subject: Character
actions.raw: If a “feature” is something likely to come up only occasionally, or even just once, then you are better off allowing it as an aspect of the inspiration and power stunt rules (see the Characteristics chap- ter), charging the player a hero point for the feature when it comes into play.

actions.subject: Character
actions.raw: The player can then choose whether or not to use earned power points to ac- quire the Feature as a regular part of the character’s traits later on.

actions.subject: Modifier

actions.subject: Ability
actions.raw: So at Growth 8, you have +8 Strength and Stamina, +4 to Intimidation, +1 Speed, but -8 to Stealth, –4 Dodge and Parry, and you are size rank 0 (around 30 feet tall).

actions.subject: Check

actions.subject: Attack
actions.raw: Rank 1 requires a Perception check (DC 20) to detect that you are Insubstantial, while 2 ranks mean you look entirely normal in Insubstantial form (which may cause opponents to waste effort on you, not knowing you are immune to their attacks, for example).

actions.subject: Effect
actions.raw: Effectively the GM gains a “free” complication against you.

actions.raw: With two or more degrees of failure, any re- newed attempt in that scene requires extra effort (see Extra Effort).

actions.subject: Check
actions.raw: MIND READING AND DECEPTION If you can interact with your subject, a successful Decep- tion check against the target’s Insight check causes the subject to consciously think about a particular piece of in- formation you’re looking for, such as a password or name, allowing you to pluck it from the subject’s mind with sur- face thoughts contact.

actions.subject: Check
actions.raw: For exam- ple, if you have one degree of contact and make another Mind Reading check, getting one degree, you now have two degrees of contact.

actions.subject: Modifier
actions.raw: Trying again after two or more degrees of fail- ure does not require extra effort for you.

actions.subject: Ability
actions.raw: As a mental sensory effect, Mind Reading has a degree of subtlety, only noticeable to the subject or to characters with an appropriate mental sense, such as Mental Awareness (see the Senses effect).

actions.subject: Modifier
actions.raw: If you have the Sensory Link extra and this flaw, you can only tap into a subject’s senses, you cannot read their thoughts or memories.

actions.subject: Attack
actions.raw: Ranged Mind Reading requires a ranged attack check in addition to the effect’s normal resistance check.

actions.subject: Character
actions.raw: For example, if an effect that removes the dazed condition is used on a staggered character (who is dazed and hindered), then the character is no longer dazed, only hindered.

actions.subject: Condition
actions.raw: Similarly, if an effect im- poses a condition that supersedes part of the combined condition, only that part changes.

actions.subject: Character
actions.raw: Another character can stabilize a dying character with a successful Treatment check (DC 15) or use of a Healing effect (see the Powers chapter).

actions.subject: Attack
actions.raw: Opponents receive a +5 circumstance bonus to close attack checks but a –5 penalty to ranged attack checks (effectively giving the prone character total cover against ranged attacks).

actions.subject: Character
actions.raw: Players can use extra effort to improve a hero’s abili- ties in exchange for the hero suffering some fatigue.

actions.subject: Character

actions.subject: Character
actions.raw: This modifier allows you to have an alternate set of traits, essentially a complete alternate character you change into, one set of traits per rank in Metamorph.

actions.subject: Effect
actions.raw: All of your forms must have your full Morph effect as well;

actions.subject: Ability
actions.raw: The GM may require certain additional com- mon traits for all of your forms, particularly mental abilities and skills, if you retain them.

actions.subject: Effect
actions.raw: If you have the Metamorph extra, then targets that resist your effect treat you as if you had your normal traits, and not those granted by your Metamorph form.

actions.subject: Ability
actions.raw: Your effective Strength for lifting and moving objects with this effect is equal to your rank.

actions.subject: Effect
actions.raw: Move Move Object generally cannot have a continuous duration, since it is an active effect and re- quires at least a modicum of attention to maintain.

actions.subject: Ability
actions.raw: Your effect can inflict damage, like an appli- cation of normal Strength with damage equal to its rank.

actions.subject: Attack
actions.raw: This includes damaging targets in grabs and making ranged “strike” attacks.

actions.subject: Check
actions.raw: Then make an opposed check of your Nullify rank and the targeted effect’s rank or the target’s Will defense, whichever is higher.

actions.raw: If you are targeting the subject of an effect rather than the effect’s user, make an opposed check of Nullify rank vs.

actions.raw: Nullify may require a Fortitude rath- er than a Will check to represent an effect resisted by health and stamina rather than strength of will.

actions.raw: Like Time Travel, the GM may require Dimension Travel be Limited, Uncontrolled, or Unreliable for player characters, or treat it solely as a plot-device rather than a defined effect.

actions.subject: Check

actions.subject: Modifier
actions.raw: Trying again after two or more degrees of fail- ure does not require extra effort for you.

actions.subject: Effect
actions.raw: If you have an Area Nullify effect, this extra al- lows you to choose who in the area is affected, nullifying some targets and not others.

actions.raw: 1 point per rank Protection shields you against damage, giving you +1 to your Toughness defense per rank.

actions.subject: Ability
actions.raw: Detect has no range and only indicates the presence or absence of something (being neither acute nor accurate).

actions.subject: Condition
actions.raw: Failure means you are surprised (although, if you have Uncanny Dodge, you are not vulnerable).

actions.subject: Check
actions.raw: Each additional time you apply this option, your range increases by an additional factor of 10, so 1 rank means you have a –1 to Perception checks per 100 feet, 2 ranks makes it –1 per 1,000 feet, and so on.

actions.subject: Effect
actions.raw: If you have the Track effect, you can track warm creatures by the faint heat trails they leave be- hind.

actions.subject: Check
actions.raw: The GM may require an Expertise skill check to understand and interpret what you see.

actions.subject: Check
actions.raw: The Gamemaster may require appropriate Perception skill checks for you to pick up on particularly detailed information, with a DC ranging from 15 to 30 or more.

actions.subject: Check
actions.raw: RANGED 1 RANK You can use a sense that normally has no range (taste or touch in humans) to make Perception checks at range, with the normal –1 per 10 feet modifier.

actions.subject: Ability
actions.raw: Generally, Precognition is best treated as a plot device for the GM to provide information to the player as suits the adventure, similar to a free use of the inspiration ability of hero points.

actions.subject: Character
actions.raw: In fact, GMs looking to limit Pre- cognition and Postcognition may wish to require extra effort or hero points to use them, or require the Uncon- trolled modifier.

actions.subject: Effect

actions.raw: For a more extended range, use Remote Sensing with this modifier.

actions.subject: Effect

actions.subject: Ability
actions.raw: SUMMON DELUXE HERO’S HANDBOOK DELUXE HERO’S HANDBOOK So at Shrinking 12, you are size rank –5 (about 6 inches tall), and have a +6 bonus to active defenses and +12 Stealth bonus, but –3 Strength, –1 speed, and –6 Intimida- tion penalties.

actions.subject: Effect
actions.raw: If you have this extra, you might also acquire a Dimensional Travel effect allowing you to shift into a sub-atomic “universe” or simi- lar realm.

actions.subject: Effect
actions.raw: A summoned minion is limited to a Power Level equal to the rank of the Sum- Summon is a useful effect;

actions.subject: Check
actions.raw: Second, Gamemasters may wish to have groups of minions use team checks (see Team Checks in The Basics chapter) rather than rolling their actions separately.

actions.subject: Attack
actions.raw: For example, instead of making eight attacks for eight different minions, the GM has seven minions aid the eighth, giving that minion a +5 bonus.

actions.subject: Character
actions.raw: Gamemasters may wish to limit the use of the Heroic extra for Summon.

actions.subject: Character
actions.raw: Treating minions the same as heroes can greatly slow down combat, especially if there are more than a couple of them, since it becomes that much harder to take them out of a fight.

actions.subject: Advantage
actions.raw: POWERS DELUXE HERO’S HANDBOOK DELUXE HERO’S HANDBOOK MUTANTS & MASTERMINDS MUTANTS & MASTERMINDS level mon effect used to create it, is subject to the normal limits, and power cannot have minions of its own, either from this effect or the Minions advantage.

actions.subject: Condition
actions.raw: Your minions are particularly independent and do not have the dazed condition, having a full set of actions each round.

actions.subject: Defense
actions.raw: They have no free will of their own and are completely under your direc- tion.

actions.subject: Condition
actions.raw: Additionally, they do not have the dazed condition and take a full set of actions each round.

actions.subject: Character
actions.raw: Do not apply the Active modifier to Heroic minions, as this modifier already includes it.

actions.raw: Ranged Affliction, Resisted by Fortitude • 2 points per rank You cause a the target to feel tremendous weariness.

actions.subject: Check
actions.raw: Targets failing the Fortitude resistance check against your effect DC become fatigued, then exhausted, and finally asleep as they succumb.

actions.raw: One degree of failure leaves the target hindered and vulnerable, while two results in the target becoming defenseless and immobilized.

actions.raw: This is part of the power’s Alternate Resistance, with no change in cost.

actions.subject: Modifier
actions.raw: It requires a stan- dard action to summon each minion unless you also have the Horde extra (see previous).

actions.subject: Character
actions.raw: In fact, the GM may wish to re- strict it to villains or non-player characters (in which case a hero earns a hero point when a villain uses this extra to avoid an effect by sacrificing a minion).

actions.subject: Check
actions.raw: Your minions get an appropriate resistance check (typically Will) against (DC 10 + Summon rank) to avoid being summoned.

actions.subject: Check
actions.raw: Targets fail- ing the Fortitude resistance check against your effect DC become dazed, stunned, and finally incapacitated, passing out from the lack of oxygen.
