modifiers.description: This modifier includes the Sense-Depen- dent flaw (see Flaws) so it cannot be applied again.
modifiers.raw: This modifier includes the Sense-Depen- dent flaw (see Flaws) so it cannot be applied again.

modifiers.description: RANGE The Area modifier interacts with different ranges as follows:
modifiers.raw: RANGE The Area modifier interacts with different ranges as follows:

modifiers.description: POWERS MUTANTS & MASTERMINDS MUTANTS & MASTERMINDS DELUXE HERO’S HANDBOOK DELUXE HERO’S HANDBOOK As with the Feature effect, a Feature extra should be sig- nificant enough to be worth at least 1 power point and not solely based on the power’s descriptors.
modifiers.raw: POWERS MUTANTS & MASTERMINDS MUTANTS & MASTERMINDS DELUXE HERO’S HANDBOOK DELUXE HERO’S HANDBOOK As with the Feature effect, a Feature extra should be sig- nificant enough to be worth at least 1 power point and not solely based on the power’s descriptors.

modifiers.description: doing so is part of its “fire” descriptor and can be equally advantageous and problematic.
modifiers.raw: doing so is part of its “fire” descriptor and can be equally advantageous and problematic.

modifiers.description: HOMING FLAT • 1 POINT PER RANK This modifier grants a ranged effect an additional oppor- tunity to hit.
modifiers.raw: HOMING FLAT • 1 POINT PER RANK This modifier grants a ranged effect an additional oppor- tunity to hit.

modifiers.description: IMPERVIOUS +1 COST PER RANK A defense with this modifier is highly resistant.
modifiers.raw: IMPERVIOUS +1 COST PER RANK A defense with this modifier is highly resistant.

modifiers.description: Any effect with a resistance difficulty modifier equal to or less than half the Impervious rank (rounded up) has no effect.
modifiers.raw: Any effect with a resistance difficulty modifier equal to or less than half the Impervious rank (rounded up) has no effect.

modifiers.description: This modifier increases an effect’s du- ration.
modifiers.raw: This modifier increases an effect’s du- ration.

modifiers.description: When applied to an instant duration effect, this modifier makes it maintainable with con- centration, taking a standard action each turn to do so.
modifiers.raw: When applied to an instant duration effect, this modifier makes it maintainable with con- centration, taking a standard action each turn to do so.

modifiers.description: When applied to a sustained duration effect, this modifier makes it continuous.
modifiers.raw: When applied to a sustained duration effect, this modifier makes it continuous.

modifiers.description: INCREASED MASS FLAT • 1 POINT PER RANK This modifier may apply to an effect that allows you to carry or affect a set amount of mass, typically a movement effect like Dimensional Travel or Teleport.
modifiers.raw: INCREASED MASS FLAT • 1 POINT PER RANK This modifier may apply to an effect that allows you to carry or affect a set amount of mass, typically a movement effect like Dimensional Travel or Teleport.

modifiers.description: This modifier increases an effect’s range.
modifiers.raw: This modifier increases an effect’s range.

modifiers.description: Applied to a close effect, this modifier makes it a ranged effect.
modifiers.raw: Applied to a close effect, this modifier makes it a ranged effect.

modifiers.description: When applied to a ranged effect, this modifier makes it perception range.
modifiers.raw: When applied to a ranged effect, this modifier makes it perception range.

modifiers.description: INCURABLE FLAT • 1 POINT Effects such as Healing and Regeneration cannot heal the damage caused by an effect with this modifier;
modifiers.raw: INCURABLE FLAT • 1 POINT Effects such as Healing and Regeneration cannot heal the damage caused by an effect with this modifier;

modifiers.description: INDIRECT FLAT • 1-4 POINTS A ranged effect with this modifier can originate from a point other than the user, ignoring cover between the user and the target, such as walls and other intervening barriers, so long as they do not provide cover between the effect’s origin point and the target.
modifiers.raw: INDIRECT FLAT • 1-4 POINTS A ranged effect with this modifier can originate from a point other than the user, ignoring cover between the user and the target, such as walls and other intervening barriers, so long as they do not provide cover between the effect’s origin point and the target.

modifiers.description: INNATE FLAT • 1 POINT An effect with this modifier is an innate part of your na- ture and unaffected by Nullify (see the Nullify effect in this chapter).
modifiers.raw: INNATE FLAT • 1 POINT An effect with this modifier is an innate part of your na- ture and unaffected by Nullify (see the Nullify effect in this chapter).

modifiers.description: If the effect is not something normal to the character’s species or type, it probably isn’t innate.
modifiers.raw: If the effect is not something normal to the character’s species or type, it probably isn’t innate.

modifiers.description: INSIDIOUS FLAT • 1 POINT This modifier is similar to the Subtle modifier (later in this section), except Insidious makes the result of an effect harder to detect rather than the effect itself.
modifiers.raw: INSIDIOUS FLAT • 1 POINT This modifier is similar to the Subtle modifier (later in this section), except Insidious makes the result of an effect harder to detect rather than the effect itself.

modifiers.description: LINKED FLAT • 0 POINTS This modifier applies to two or more effects, linking them together so they only work in conjunction as one.
modifiers.raw: LINKED FLAT • 0 POINTS This modifier applies to two or more effects, linking them together so they only work in conjunction as one.

modifiers.description: The action required to use the combined effects is the longest of its components and they use a single attack check (if one is required) and resistance check (if both effects use the same type of check).
modifiers.raw: The action required to use the combined effects is the longest of its components and they use a single attack check (if one is required) and resistance check (if both effects use the same type of check).

modifiers.description: Different Alternate Effects cannot be Linked since they can’t be used at the same time by definition.
modifiers.raw: Different Alternate Effects cannot be Linked since they can’t be used at the same time by definition.

modifiers.description: This modifier does not change the cost of the component effects;
modifiers.raw: This modifier does not change the cost of the component effects;

modifiers.description: (To do these things, Cap might take the stand-alone effects as Alternate Effects.) MULTIATTACK +1 COST PER RANK A Multiattack effect allows you to hit multiple targets, or a single target multiple times, in the same standard action.
modifiers.raw: (To do these things, Cap might take the stand-alone effects as Alternate Effects.) MULTIATTACK +1 COST PER RANK A Multiattack effect allows you to hit multiple targets, or a single target multiple times, in the same standard action.

modifiers.description: This circumstance bonus does not count against power level limits.
modifiers.raw: This circumstance bonus does not count against power level limits.

modifiers.description: PRECISE FLAT • 1 POINT You can use a Precise effect to perform tasks requiring delicacy and fine control, such as using Precise Damage to spot-weld or carve your initials, Precise Move Object to type or pick a lock, Precise Environment to match a par- ticular temperature exactly, and so forth.
modifiers.raw: PRECISE FLAT • 1 POINT You can use a Precise effect to perform tasks requiring delicacy and fine control, such as using Precise Damage to spot-weld or carve your initials, Precise Move Object to type or pick a lock, Precise Environment to match a par- ticular temperature exactly, and so forth.

modifiers.description: REACH FLAT • 1 POINT PER RANK Each time you apply this modifier to a close range effect, you extend its reach by 5 feet.
modifiers.raw: REACH FLAT • 1 POINT PER RANK Each time you apply this modifier to a close range effect, you extend its reach by 5 feet.

modifiers.description: This modifier changes an effect’s required action from a standard or free to a re- action, occurring automatically when a specific triggering event occurs.
modifiers.raw: This modifier changes an effect’s required action from a standard or free to a re- action, occurring automatically when a specific triggering event occurs.

modifiers.description: This is similar to the Triggered modifier (later in this section) but reaction effects do not need to be “set,” originate from their user, and can function repeatedly, so long as their triggering requirement is met.
modifiers.raw: This is similar to the Triggered modifier (later in this section) but reaction effects do not need to be “set,” originate from their user, and can function repeatedly, so long as their triggering requirement is met.

modifiers.description: For the ability to choose to have some triggering cir- cumstances activate the effect and some not, apply the Selective modifier as well.
modifiers.raw: For the ability to choose to have some triggering cir- cumstances activate the effect and some not, apply the Selective modifier as well.

modifiers.description: If Doctor Shock possessed the ability to have his aura only damage people and things he wants it to damage, he would need to have the Selec- tive modifier applied to the effect as well.
modifiers.raw: If Doctor Shock possessed the ability to have his aura only damage people and things he wants it to damage, he would need to have the Selec- tive modifier applied to the effect as well.

modifiers.description: The Reaction modifier applies +1 cost per rank to effects with a default action of free, +3 cost per rank to effects with a default standard action.
modifiers.raw: The Reaction modifier applies +1 cost per rank to effects with a default action of free, +3 cost per rank to effects with a default standard action.

modifiers.description: RICOCHET FLAT • 1 POINT PER RANK You can ricochet or bounce an attack effect with this modifier off of a solid surface to change its direction.
modifiers.raw: RICOCHET FLAT • 1 POINT PER RANK You can ricochet or bounce an attack effect with this modifier off of a solid surface to change its direction.

modifiers.description: SECONDARY EFFECT +1 COST PER RANK An instant duration effect with this modifier affects the target once immediately (when the effect is used) and then once again on the following round, at the end of the attacker’s turn.
modifiers.raw: SECONDARY EFFECT +1 COST PER RANK An instant duration effect with this modifier affects the target once immediately (when the effect is used) and then once again on the following round, at the end of the attacker’s turn.

modifiers.description: For a degree of selectivity with non-resistible effects, use the Precise modifier.
modifiers.raw: For a degree of selectivity with non-resistible effects, use the Precise modifier.

modifiers.description: SLEEP +0 COST PER RANK When this modifier is applied to an effect that causes the incapacitated condition, the effect leaves them asleep whenever it would normally render them incapacitated.
modifiers.raw: SLEEP +0 COST PER RANK When this modifier is applied to an effect that causes the incapacitated condition, the effect leaves them asleep whenever it would normally render them incapacitated.

modifiers.description: SPLIT FLAT • 1 POINT PER RANK With this modifier, a resistible effect that works on one tar- get can split between two.
modifiers.raw: SPLIT FLAT • 1 POINT PER RANK With this modifier, a resistible effect that works on one tar- get can split between two.

modifiers.description: Each additional rank of this modifier allows the power to split an additional time, so rank 2 allows an effect to split among three targets, then four, and so forth.
modifiers.raw: Each additional rank of this modifier allows the power to split an additional time, so rank 2 allows an effect to split among three targets, then four, and so forth.

modifiers.description: SUSTAINED +0 COST PER RANK Applied to a permanent duration effect, this modifier makes it sustained duration, requiring a free action to use (rather than none, like other permanent effects).
modifiers.raw: SUSTAINED +0 COST PER RANK Applied to a permanent duration effect, this modifier makes it sustained duration, requiring a free action to use (rather than none, like other permanent effects).

modifiers.description: SELECTIVE +1 COST PER RANK A resistible effect with this extra is discriminating, allow- ing you to decide who is and is not affected by it.
modifiers.raw: SELECTIVE +1 COST PER RANK A resistible effect with this extra is discriminating, allow- ing you to decide who is and is not affected by it.

modifiers.description: POWERS MUTANTS & MASTERMINDS MUTANTS & MASTERMINDS DELUXE HERO’S HANDBOOK DELUXE HERO’S HANDBOOK sponse to a particular danger, after a set amount of time, in response to a particular event, and so forth—chosen when you apply the modifier.
modifiers.raw: POWERS MUTANTS & MASTERMINDS MUTANTS & MASTERMINDS DELUXE HERO’S HANDBOOK DELUXE HERO’S HANDBOOK sponse to a particular danger, after a set amount of time, in response to a particular event, and so forth—chosen when you apply the modifier.

modifiers.description: A Triggered effect is good for one use per rank in this modifier.
modifiers.raw: A Triggered effect is good for one use per rank in this modifier.

modifiers.description: VARIABLE DESCRIPTOR FLAT • 1-2 POINTS You can change the descriptors of an effect with this modi- fier, varying them as a free action once per round.
modifiers.raw: VARIABLE DESCRIPTOR FLAT • 1-2 POINTS You can change the descriptors of an effect with this modi- fier, varying them as a free action once per round.

modifiers.description: With rank 2, you can apply any of a broad group, such as any mental, magical, or technological descriptor.
modifiers.raw: With rank 2, you can apply any of a broad group, such as any mental, magical, or technological descriptor.

modifiers.description: The GM decides if a given descriptor is appropriate in con- junction with a particular effect and this modifier.
modifiers.raw: The GM decides if a given descriptor is appropriate in con- junction with a particular effect and this modifier.

modifiers.description: CONCENTRATION -1 COST PER RANK Applied to a sustained duration effect, this modifier makes it concentration duration, requiring a standard ac- tion, rather than a free action, each turn to maintain.
modifiers.raw: CONCENTRATION -1 COST PER RANK Applied to a sustained duration effect, this modifier makes it concentration duration, requiring a standard ac- tion, rather than a free action, each turn to maintain.

modifiers.description: As a gen- eral rule, the effect must lose about half its usefulness to qualify for this modifier.
modifiers.raw: As a gen- eral rule, the effect must lose about half its usefulness to qualify for this modifier.

modifiers.description: If the ef- fect’s default range is not close, apply the Close modifier as well.
modifiers.raw: If the ef- fect’s default range is not close, apply the Close modifier as well.

modifiers.description: Each increase in the required action from that effect’s base action type (free to move, for example) is a –1 cost per rank modifier.
modifiers.raw: Each increase in the required action from that effect’s base action type (free to move, for example) is a –1 cost per rank modifier.

modifiers.description: NOTICEABLE FLAT • -1 POINT A continuous or permanent effect with this modifier is noticeable in some sort of way (see Noticing Power Ef- fects at the start of the chapter).
modifiers.raw: NOTICEABLE FLAT • -1 POINT A continuous or permanent effect with this modifier is noticeable in some sort of way (see Noticing Power Ef- fects at the start of the chapter).

modifiers.description: As with Features, the GM should ensure a Quirk is truly a flaw (albeit a minor one) and not simply part of the pow- er’s descriptors.
modifiers.raw: As with Features, the GM should ensure a Quirk is truly a flaw (albeit a minor one) and not simply part of the pow- er’s descriptors.

modifiers.description: For example, the fact that an attack with a “sonic” descriptor likely will not travel through a vacuum is not a Quirk, simply part of the “sonic” descriptor (es- pecially since the attack may be enhanced by a medium such as water).
modifiers.raw: For example, the fact that an attack with a “sonic” descriptor likely will not travel through a vacuum is not a Quirk, simply part of the “sonic” descriptor (es- pecially since the attack may be enhanced by a medium such as water).

modifiers.description: Large ammo or fuel capacities, which only occasionally run out or inconvenience the character, are better handled as descriptors and occasional complica- tions when they actually prove problematic.
modifiers.raw: Large ammo or fuel capacities, which only occasionally run out or inconvenience the character, are better handled as descriptors and occasional complica- tions when they actually prove problematic.

modifiers.description: POWERS 203203 MUTANTS & MASTERMINDS MUTANTS & MASTERMINDS DELUXE HERO’S HANDBOOK DELUXE HERO’S HANDBOOK DESCRIPTORS Descriptors help to bring a collection of effects and modifiers to life, differentiating them from similar (or even identical) configurations and making them into distinct powers.
modifiers.raw: POWERS 203203 MUTANTS & MASTERMINDS MUTANTS & MASTERMINDS DELUXE HERO’S HANDBOOK DELUXE HERO’S HANDBOOK DESCRIPTORS Descriptors help to bring a collection of effects and modifiers to life, differentiating them from similar (or even identical) configurations and making them into distinct powers.

modifiers.description: Although descriptors don’t always have significant game effects in MUTANTS & MASTERMINDS, they’re perhaps the most important element in providing color and character to the powers of heroes and villains.
modifiers.raw: Although descriptors don’t always have significant game effects in MUTANTS & MASTERMINDS, they’re perhaps the most important element in providing color and character to the powers of heroes and villains.

modifiers.description: Descriptors do have some affect on game play.
modifiers.raw: Descriptors do have some affect on game play.

modifiers.description: In particular, descriptors often govern how certain effects interact with each other, serving as convenient shorthand to help define an effect’s parameters.
modifiers.raw: In particular, descriptors often govern how certain effects interact with each other, serving as convenient shorthand to help define an effect’s parameters.

modifiers.description: For example, Immunity and Nullify work against effects with specific descriptors;
modifiers.raw: For example, Immunity and Nullify work against effects with specific descriptors;

modifiers.description: if they were limited solely to things like effect type, it would leave out a tremendous range of options, like “Immunity to Fire” or “Nullify Mutant Powers,” which are important to the source material.
modifiers.raw: if they were limited solely to things like effect type, it would leave out a tremendous range of options, like “Immunity to Fire” or “Nullify Mutant Powers,” which are important to the source material.

modifiers.description: TYPES OF DESCRIPTORS Descriptors come in many different forms.
modifiers.raw: TYPES OF DESCRIPTORS Descriptors come in many different forms.

modifiers.description: some descriptors fall into more than one category, while others might not fall into any of these categories, being unique to that particular character or power.
modifiers.raw: some descriptors fall into more than one category, while others might not fall into any of these categories, being unique to that particular character or power.

modifiers.description: Still, the following are the major types of descriptors suited to MUTANTS & MAS- TERMINDS powers, and things to consider when creating or choosing powers for a character.
modifiers.raw: Still, the following are the major types of descriptors suited to MUTANTS & MAS- TERMINDS powers, and things to consider when creating or choosing powers for a character.

modifiers.description: ORIGIN A descriptor may relate to the origin of a power, where it comes from or what granted it to the character.
modifiers.raw: ORIGIN A descriptor may relate to the origin of a power, where it comes from or what granted it to the character.

modifiers.description: Ex- amples of origin descriptors include:
modifiers.raw: Ex- amples of origin descriptors include:

modifiers.description: Comic book style superpowers answer this question with vague descriptors, since the kind of real-world energy re- quired for many powers is staggering, requiring all super- humans to be living fusion reactors!
modifiers.raw: Comic book style superpowers answer this question with vague descriptors, since the kind of real-world energy re- quired for many powers is staggering, requiring all super- humans to be living fusion reactors!

modifiers.description: While this may well be the case in your own setting, the assumption is that power source is just another descriptor in most MUTANTS & MASTERMINDS games.
modifiers.raw: While this may well be the case in your own setting, the assumption is that power source is just another descriptor in most MUTANTS & MASTERMINDS games.

modifiers.description: Source descriptors influence the effects of certain powers, such as Nullify Magic Powers, which can counter powers with a magical source, whether or not their effects are magical.
modifiers.raw: Source descriptors influence the effects of certain powers, such as Nullify Magic Powers, which can counter powers with a magical source, whether or not their effects are magical.

modifiers.description: Result descriptors tend to be fairly broad, given the potential range of results avail- able to effects in the game.
modifiers.raw: Result descriptors tend to be fairly broad, given the potential range of results avail- able to effects in the game.

modifiers.description: Some powers may not have or need result descriptors;
modifiers.raw: Some powers may not have or need result descriptors;

modifiers.description: However, “an in- duced trance where the human brain becomes capable of accepting neurolin- guistic programming inputs” is also a valid descriptor for that same effect.
modifiers.raw: However, “an in- duced trance where the human brain becomes capable of accepting neurolin- guistic programming inputs” is also a valid descriptor for that same effect.

modifiers.description: Like medium descriptors, result descriptors may or may not match others the power al- ready has.
modifiers.raw: Like medium descriptors, result descriptors may or may not match others the power al- ready has.

modifiers.description: it has an invented origin (someone designed and built it), a techno- logical source (it’s a technological device with a battery), uses a energy medium (an electrical shock), and results in an electrical overload of the tar- get’s nervous system (the result descriptor for its Affliction effect).
modifiers.raw: it has an invented origin (someone designed and built it), a techno- logical source (it’s a technological device with a battery), uses a energy medium (an electrical shock), and results in an electrical overload of the tar- get’s nervous system (the result descriptor for its Affliction effect).

modifiers.description: APPLYING DESCRIPTORS Applying descriptors to a power is as simple as describing what the power is and how it works:
modifiers.raw: APPLYING DESCRIPTORS Applying descriptors to a power is as simple as describing what the power is and how it works:

modifiers.description: So, for example, “area” is not a descriptor, it is an extra you apply to allow a power to affect an area rather than a single target.
modifiers.raw: So, for example, “area” is not a descriptor, it is an extra you apply to allow a power to affect an area rather than a single target.

modifiers.description: POWERS MUTANTS & MASTERMINDS MUTANTS & MASTERMINDS CREATIVE USES OF DESCRIPTORS DELUXE HERO’S HANDBOOK DELUXE HERO’S HANDBOOK In many instances, players come up with creative uses for their characters’ descriptors.
modifiers.raw: POWERS MUTANTS & MASTERMINDS MUTANTS & MASTERMINDS CREATIVE USES OF DESCRIPTORS DELUXE HERO’S HANDBOOK DELUXE HERO’S HANDBOOK In many instances, players come up with creative uses for their characters’ descriptors.

modifiers.description: Creative uses of descriptors with no real game effect are freebies:
modifiers.raw: Creative uses of descriptors with no real game effect are freebies:

modifiers.description: Situations where creative uses of descriptors have a significant game effect can be handled as power stunts:
modifiers.raw: Situations where creative uses of descriptors have a significant game effect can be handled as power stunts:

modifiers.description: pick the effect that best suits the desired outcome and treat it as an Alternate Effect of the power the hero wants to use, with descrip- tors assigned as appropriate.
modifiers.raw: pick the effect that best suits the desired outcome and treat it as an Alternate Effect of the power the hero wants to use, with descrip- tors assigned as appropriate.

modifiers.description: APPLYING DESCRIPTORS IN PLAY While descriptors are generally applied to powers when those powers are defined (that is, when a character is created), in some cases, certain descriptors may be left unspecified and defined during play.
modifiers.raw: APPLYING DESCRIPTORS IN PLAY While descriptors are generally applied to powers when those powers are defined (that is, when a character is created), in some cases, certain descriptors may be left unspecified and defined during play.

modifiers.description: This can either be because nobody thought to define the descriptor in ad- vance, or it was deliberately left vague, to be filled-in later.
modifiers.raw: This can either be because nobody thought to define the descriptor in ad- vance, or it was deliberately left vague, to be filled-in later.

modifiers.description: The GM agrees and so the heroine’s powers have no origin or source descriptors.
modifiers.raw: The GM agrees and so the heroine’s powers have no origin or source descriptors.

modifiers.description: Applying descriptors in play gives you a lot of flexibility, letting you handle certain things “on the fly” rather than having to describe every aspect of a character in excruci- ating detail beforehand.
modifiers.raw: Applying descriptors in play gives you a lot of flexibility, letting you handle certain things “on the fly” rather than having to describe every aspect of a character in excruci- ating detail beforehand.

modifiers.description: The key tool for handling the ap- plication of descriptors in play is the use of hero points.
modifiers.raw: The key tool for handling the ap- plication of descriptors in play is the use of hero points.

modifiers.description: If applying a new descriptor is a setback for the hero, then award the player a hero point, just like any other setback (see Complications, page 30).
modifiers.raw: If applying a new descriptor is a setback for the hero, then award the player a hero point, just like any other setback (see Complications, page 30).

modifiers.description: If the new descriptor is chosen by the player and gives the hero a minor advan- tage, you might ask the player to pay a hero point for the privilege, although you can balance this with an immedi- ate hero point award for the clever idea, if you want (mak- ing the hero point a token expenditure).
modifiers.raw: If the new descriptor is chosen by the player and gives the hero a minor advan- tage, you might ask the player to pay a hero point for the privilege, although you can balance this with an immedi- ate hero point award for the clever idea, if you want (mak- ing the hero point a token expenditure).

modifiers.description: If it’s neither, then there’s no hero point cost, just apply the descriptor.
modifiers.raw: If it’s neither, then there’s no hero point cost, just apply the descriptor.

modifiers.description: CHANGING DESCRIPTORS IN PLAY On some occasions, you or a player may wish to change a particular descriptor during the course of the game, re- moving an existing descriptor and possibly replacing it with another one.
modifiers.raw: CHANGING DESCRIPTORS IN PLAY On some occasions, you or a player may wish to change a particular descriptor during the course of the game, re- moving an existing descriptor and possibly replacing it with another one.

modifiers.description: So long as the change doesn’t contradict any pre- viously introduced information, this is no different than applying a descriptor in play, and should be handled in the same way.
modifiers.raw: So long as the change doesn’t contradict any pre- viously introduced information, this is no different than applying a descriptor in play, and should be handled in the same way.

modifiers.description: Changing descriptors is best handled as a plot device, much like re-allocating power points and redesigning characters (see page 26 for details).
modifiers.raw: Changing descriptors is best handled as a plot device, much like re-allocating power points and redesigning characters (see page 26 for details).

modifiers.description: Like with defining descriptors in play, if a change in descriptors through GM Fiat constitutes a setback, the GM should award the player a hero point.
modifiers.raw: Like with defining descriptors in play, if a change in descriptors through GM Fiat constitutes a setback, the GM should award the player a hero point.

modifiers.description: Temporarily changing a descriptor can be a use of extra ef- fort, like any other power stunt.
modifiers.raw: Temporarily changing a descriptor can be a use of extra ef- fort, like any other power stunt.

modifiers.description: This is like any other power stunt and the changed or additional descriptors are an important part of the stunt.
modifiers.raw: This is like any other power stunt and the changed or additional descriptors are an important part of the stunt.

modifiers.description: Sometimes a power stunt may change nothing but an effect’s descriptor(s), such as changing a Damage effect from laser-light to a gamma-ray “graser” or from heat to cold.
modifiers.raw: Sometimes a power stunt may change nothing but an effect’s descriptor(s), such as changing a Damage effect from laser-light to a gamma-ray “graser” or from heat to cold.

modifiers.description: The GM decides what constitutes a reasonable change in descriptors for a power stunt, based on the power’s existing descriptors and effects.
modifiers.raw: The GM decides what constitutes a reasonable change in descriptors for a power stunt, based on the power’s existing descriptors and effects.

modifiers.description: ALTERNATE EQUIPMENT Just as with power effects, there is a diminishing value in having multiple items with a similar function, or a single piece of equipment with multiple functions, usable only one at a time.
modifiers.raw: ALTERNATE EQUIPMENT Just as with power effects, there is a diminishing value in having multiple items with a similar function, or a single piece of equipment with multiple functions, usable only one at a time.

modifiers.description: Equipment can have the Alternate Effect modifier (see the Extras section of the Powers chapter), such as a weapon capable of different modes of opera- tion, or a reconfigurable tool.
modifiers.raw: Equipment can have the Alternate Effect modifier (see the Extras section of the Powers chapter), such as a weapon capable of different modes of opera- tion, or a reconfigurable tool.

modifiers.description: Characters can also have Alternate Equipment, an array of items usable only one at a time.
modifiers.raw: Characters can also have Alternate Equipment, an array of items usable only one at a time.

modifiers.description: Alternate Equipment can also include things like an arsenal of weapons the char- acter can swap out, providing different sets of weapons, with only a limited number usable at once.
modifiers.raw: Alternate Equipment can also include things like an arsenal of weapons the char- acter can swap out, providing different sets of weapons, with only a limited number usable at once.

modifiers.description: Note that equipment with a cost of 1 equipment point doesn’t really need to be acquired as Alternate Equip- ment, since there’s no change in cost.
modifiers.raw: Note that equipment with a cost of 1 equipment point doesn’t really need to be acquired as Alternate Equip- ment, since there’s no change in cost.

modifiers.description: The other items cost 1 point each for Alternate Equipment, making the total equipment point cost of the utility belt 25 equipment points, or 5 power points (for 5 ranks of the Equipment advantage).
modifiers.raw: The other items cost 1 point each for Alternate Equipment, making the total equipment point cost of the utility belt 25 equipment points, or 5 power points (for 5 ranks of the Equipment advantage).

modifiers.description: Characters pay this cost from their equipment points to have a weapon of this type as part of their regular equipment.
modifiers.raw: Characters pay this cost from their equipment points to have a weapon of this type as part of their regular equipment.

modifiers.description: A flamethrower shoots a stream or arc of fire Damage as Cone or Line Area and can switch between settings as an Alternate Effect.
modifiers.raw: A flamethrower shoots a stream or arc of fire Damage as Cone or Line Area and can switch between settings as an Alternate Effect.

modifiers.description: A common throwing weapon for heroes, a thrown boomerang returns to the thrower’s hand, ready to be thrown again on the next round (less a Feature and more a special descriptor).
modifiers.raw: A common throwing weapon for heroes, a thrown boomerang returns to the thrower’s hand, ready to be thrown again on the next round (less a Feature and more a special descriptor).

modifiers.description: This is best handled as an Alternate Effect of the shield, mean- ing you can’t use it both offensively and defensively at the same time!
modifiers.raw: This is best handled as an Alternate Effect of the shield, mean- ing you can’t use it both offensively and defensively at the same time!

modifiers.description: Features are “optional extras” for vehicles and cost 1 point each.
modifiers.raw: Features are “optional extras” for vehicles and cost 1 point each.

modifiers.description: Some vehicles may also have Concealment from auditory senses or things such as radar, giving them a “stealth mode.” 4 points (normal vision or all of another sense type) or 8 points (all visual senses).
modifiers.raw: Some vehicles may also have Concealment from auditory senses or things such as radar, giving them a “stealth mode.” 4 points (normal vision or all of another sense type) or 8 points (all visual senses).

modifiers.description: ALTERNATE VEHICLES Just like Alternate Equipment, characters may have mul- tiple vehicles.
modifiers.raw: ALTERNATE VEHICLES Just like Alternate Equipment, characters may have mul- tiple vehicles.

modifiers.description: These are generally Alternate Equipment by definition, since it’s difficult to drive or pilot more than one vehicle at a time!
modifiers.raw: These are generally Alternate Equipment by definition, since it’s difficult to drive or pilot more than one vehicle at a time!

modifiers.description: A character can even have multiple bases of operation (see Alternate Headquarters later in this section).
modifiers.raw: A character can even have multiple bases of operation (see Alternate Headquarters later in this section).

modifiers.description: POWER LEVEL Some features refer to a headquarters’ power level.
modifiers.raw: POWER LEVEL Some features refer to a headquarters’ power level.

modifiers.description: For player characters, this is the power level of the series over- all.
modifiers.raw: For player characters, this is the power level of the series over- all.

modifiers.description: For non-player characters, particularly villains, this is the base-owner’s effective power level, or whatever pow- er level the GM wishes to set, using the series power level as a guideline.
modifiers.raw: For non-player characters, particularly villains, this is the base-owner’s effective power level, or whatever pow- er level the GM wishes to set, using the series power level as a guideline.

modifiers.description: Gen- erally, a combat simulator has a suite of equipment that can simulate any appropriate attack effect at a rank up to the HQ power level.
modifiers.raw: Gen- erally, a combat simulator has a suite of equipment that can simulate any appropriate attack effect at a rank up to the HQ power level.

modifiers.description: A defense system can have any attack effect with a cost no greater than twice the HQ power level.
modifiers.raw: A defense system can have any attack effect with a cost no greater than twice the HQ power level.

modifiers.description: Their attack bonus is equal to the power level.
modifiers.raw: Their attack bonus is equal to the power level.

modifiers.description: The ef- fect cannot have a total cost greater than twice the HQ power level and cannot exceed the power level limits.
modifiers.raw: The ef- fect cannot have a total cost greater than twice the HQ power level and cannot exceed the power level limits.

modifiers.description: The cells are equipped with Nul- lify devices (ranked at the HQ power level) or their basic Toughness is increased by 50%, which option should be agreed upon by both player and GM (both options for two features).
modifiers.raw: The cells are equipped with Nul- lify devices (ranked at the HQ power level) or their basic Toughness is increased by 50%, which option should be agreed upon by both player and GM (both options for two features).

modifiers.description: An infirmary can provide treatment for a number of characters equal to the base’s power level at one time and it can be assumed to have the necessary facilities to handle any unusual physiology of the base’s owner(s).
modifiers.raw: An infirmary can provide treatment for a number of characters equal to the base’s power level at one time and it can be assumed to have the necessary facilities to handle any unusual physiology of the base’s owner(s).

modifiers.description: This is usually a number of residents equal to the HQ’s power level comfortably (pos- sibly more, at the GM’s discretion).
modifiers.raw: This is usually a number of residents equal to the HQ’s power level comfortably (pos- sibly more, at the GM’s discretion).

modifiers.description: This generally lasts for a number of hours equal to the HQ’s power level.
modifiers.raw: This generally lasts for a number of hours equal to the HQ’s power level.

modifiers.description: In the event that a character has more than one headquar- ters, such as hidden bases scattered around the world, the others are treated as Alternate Equipment:
modifiers.raw: In the event that a character has more than one headquar- ters, such as hidden bases scattered around the world, the others are treated as Alternate Equipment:

modifiers.description: Essentially, it recovers like a character The only exception to the Alternate Headquarters guide- line is a shared headquarters (previously), the characters sharing the HQ all contribute to its cost and pay for any personal headquarters separately.
modifiers.raw: Essentially, it recovers like a character The only exception to the Alternate Headquarters guide- line is a shared headquarters (previously), the characters sharing the HQ all contribute to its cost and pay for any personal headquarters separately.

modifiers.description: Constructs are subject to the same power level limits as other characters and the Gamemaster should require con- structs controlled by the players to observe these limits.
modifiers.raw: Constructs are subject to the same power level limits as other characters and the Gamemaster should require con- structs controlled by the players to observe these limits.

modifiers.description: Non-player character constructs have their power level determined the same as other NPCs.
modifiers.raw: Non-player character constructs have their power level determined the same as other NPCs.

modifiers.description: d20 + initiative modifier The initiative check determines what order characters act in, counting down from highest check result to low- est.
modifiers.raw: d20 + initiative modifier The initiative check determines what order characters act in, counting down from highest check result to low- est.

modifiers.description: DETAILS Go through the limits listed under Power Level in this chapter and make sure your hero’s traits all fit within them.
modifiers.raw: DETAILS Go through the limits listed under Power Level in this chapter and make sure your hero’s traits all fit within them.

modifiers.description: The GM should check again to make sure your power points are spent and added up correctly, your hero follows the power level guidelines and any other guidelines set for the series, and that the character is generally complete and suited to the overall game.
modifiers.raw: The GM should check again to make sure your power points are spent and added up correctly, your hero follows the power level guidelines and any other guidelines set for the series, and that the character is generally complete and suited to the overall game.

modifiers.description: STARTING POWER POINTS The game’s power level provides a guideline for how many power points you get initially to design your character, as shown on the Starting Power Points table.
modifiers.raw: STARTING POWER POINTS The game’s power level provides a guideline for how many power points you get initially to design your character, as shown on the Starting Power Points table.

modifiers.description: POWER LEVEL Power level is an overall measure of effectiveness and power, primarily combat ability, but also generally what sort of tasks a character can be expected to accomplish on a routine basis (see Routine Checks in The Basics chapter).
modifiers.raw: POWER LEVEL Power level is an overall measure of effectiveness and power, primarily combat ability, but also generally what sort of tasks a character can be expected to accomplish on a routine basis (see Routine Checks in The Basics chapter).

modifiers.description: This option can represent a “lucky” attack that does something completely different, like blinding a target, or imposing a condition such as those found under the Affliction effect.
modifiers.raw: This option can represent a “lucky” attack that does something completely different, like blinding a target, or imposing a condition such as those found under the Affliction effect.

modifiers.description: see the Alternate Effect modi- fier for details.
modifiers.raw: see the Alternate Effect modi- fier for details.

modifiers.description: Glances at Princess’ Strength of 12, and the +5 critical hit DC modifier, making the Toughness resistance DC (15 + 12 + 5) or 32.
modifiers.raw: Glances at Princess’ Strength of 12, and the +5 critical hit DC modifier, making the Toughness resistance DC (15 + 12 + 5) or 32.

modifiers.description: At long range (up to rank x 100 feet), the attack check has a –5 circumstance modifier.
modifiers.raw: At long range (up to rank x 100 feet), the attack check has a –5 circumstance modifier.

modifiers.description: CONDITIONS A failed resistance check against an attack imposes one or more conditions on the target, depending on the type of effect and the degree of failure.
modifiers.raw: CONDITIONS A failed resistance check against an attack imposes one or more conditions on the target, depending on the type of effect and the degree of failure.

modifiers.description: Ma- neuvers are optional, you choose which, if any, apply to your action(s) when you declare them.
modifiers.raw: Ma- neuvers are optional, you choose which, if any, apply to your action(s) when you declare them.

modifiers.description: ACCURATE ATTACK DEMORALIZE When you make an attack, you can take a penalty of up to –2 on the effect modifier of the attack and add the same number (up to +2) to your attack bonus.
modifiers.raw: ACCURATE ATTACK DEMORALIZE When you make an attack, you can take a penalty of up to –2 on the effect modifier of the attack and add the same number (up to +2) to your attack bonus.

modifiers.description: The Gamemaster may limit your base slam attack damage (before applying circumstance modifiers) by the series power level.
modifiers.raw: The Gamemaster may limit your base slam attack damage (before applying circumstance modifiers) by the series power level.

modifiers.description: If a base damage rank of 10 is too high for the series, the GM may impose a lower limit on Slingshot’s slam attack damage, applying the +1 modifier for the full speed move to the lowered rank.
modifiers.raw: If a base damage rank of 10 is too high for the series, the GM may impose a lower limit on Slingshot’s slam attack damage, applying the +1 modifier for the full speed move to the lowered rank.

modifiers.description: The GM can also grant you a surprise attack for an unusual maneuver that catches the target off-guard, such as an Indirect attack (see the Indirect modifier for more).
modifiers.raw: The GM can also grant you a surprise attack for an unusual maneuver that catches the target off-guard, such as an Indirect attack (see the Indirect modifier for more).

modifiers.description: (See Hero Points, page 20.) CIRCUMSTANCE MODIFIERS A good guideline to keep in mind is the chance of an aver- age character (with a modifier of 0) succeeding at an av- erage task (DC 10) is just over 50% (55% to be exact).
modifiers.raw: (See Hero Points, page 20.) CIRCUMSTANCE MODIFIERS A good guideline to keep in mind is the chance of an aver- age character (with a modifier of 0) succeeding at an av- erage task (DC 10) is just over 50% (55% to be exact).

modifiers.description: If things are particularly good or bad, up the circumstance modifier to +/–5.
modifiers.raw: If things are particularly good or bad, up the circumstance modifier to +/–5.

modifiers.description: Note that, practically speaking, a major cir- cumstance modifier effectively shifts a check up or down a degree of difficulty, as shown on the Difficulty Class Examples table.
modifiers.raw: Note that, practically speaking, a major cir- cumstance modifier effectively shifts a check up or down a degree of difficulty, as shown on the Difficulty Class Examples table.

modifiers.description: Likewise, a major modifier effectively changes the degree of a graded check by one (see Grad- ed Checks in Chapter 1).
modifiers.raw: Likewise, a major modifier effectively changes the degree of a graded check by one (see Grad- ed Checks in Chapter 1).

modifiers.description: Of course, If the situation is stressful—such as the midst of combat—then a routine check is not an option, and a lower DC can provide heroes with a reasonably high chance of success with just a small chance of failure for dramatic purposes.
modifiers.raw: Of course, If the situation is stressful—such as the midst of combat—then a routine check is not an option, and a lower DC can provide heroes with a reasonably high chance of success with just a small chance of failure for dramatic purposes.

modifiers.description: In these cases, feel free to While the MUTANTS & MASTERMINDS game rules provide general outcomes for character actions, in terms of relative success or failure, what the rules do not provide is the “flavor” of those actions, the descriptions and visuals of exactly how they happen.
modifiers.raw: In these cases, feel free to While the MUTANTS & MASTERMINDS game rules provide general outcomes for character actions, in terms of relative success or failure, what the rules do not provide is the “flavor” of those actions, the descriptions and visuals of exactly how they happen.

modifiers.description: Likewise, if a power level 10 hero is going to take out a PL 3 thug, you don’t have to make all the rolls.
modifiers.raw: Likewise, if a power level 10 hero is going to take out a PL 3 thug, you don’t have to make all the rolls.

modifiers.description: GAMEMASTERING appropriate type of check, a Difficulty Class, and make a roll to see if the character succeeds or not!
modifiers.raw: GAMEMASTERING appropriate type of check, a Difficulty Class, and make a roll to see if the character succeeds or not!

modifiers.description: CHECKS Actions in MUTANTS & MASTERMINDS are all resolved through checks, a roll of a 20-sided die, plus a modifier.
modifiers.raw: CHECKS Actions in MUTANTS & MASTERMINDS are all resolved through checks, a roll of a 20-sided die, plus a modifier.

modifiers.description: RESISTANCE CHECKS Avoiding an effect is a resistance check, with a Difficulty Class of 10 + the effect’s modifier or rank.
modifiers.raw: RESISTANCE CHECKS Avoiding an effect is a resistance check, with a Difficulty Class of 10 + the effect’s modifier or rank.

modifiers.description: UNBALANCED HEROES The power level guidelines built into the rules help en- sure MUTANTS & MASTERMINDS characters of the same power level are at least in the same ballpark in terms of overall effectiveness.
modifiers.raw: UNBALANCED HEROES The power level guidelines built into the rules help en- sure MUTANTS & MASTERMINDS characters of the same power level are at least in the same ballpark in terms of overall effectiveness.

modifiers.description: Note that the GM has the authority to say “no” to a particu- lar power effect or other trait, even if it is perfectly “legal” in terms of the game rules and the power level of the se- ries.
modifiers.raw: Note that the GM has the authority to say “no” to a particu- lar power effect or other trait, even if it is perfectly “legal” in terms of the game rules and the power level of the se- ries.

modifiers.description: This is a particularly harsh option to impose, but heroes still gain the benefit of hero points to help them avoid this fate.
modifiers.raw: This is a particularly harsh option to impose, but heroes still gain the benefit of hero points to help them avoid this fate.

modifiers.description: Another option is to allow the players to run the guest stars instead of, or in addition to, their regular characters.
modifiers.raw: Another option is to allow the players to run the guest stars instead of, or in addition to, their regular characters.

modifiers.description: Players spend their heroes’ awarded power points be- tween adventures to improve the heroes’ traits, limited only by the series power level.
modifiers.raw: Players spend their heroes’ awarded power points be- tween adventures to improve the heroes’ traits, limited only by the series power level.

modifiers.description: They also can choose to save up unspent power points, waiting until the series power level increases, in order to spend them to improve a trait already at its maximum rank.
modifiers.raw: They also can choose to save up unspent power points, waiting until the series power level increases, in order to spend them to improve a trait already at its maximum rank.

modifiers.description: GAMEMASTERING MUTANTS & MASTERMINDS MUTANTS & MASTERMINDS INCREASING POWER LEVEL WEALTH DELUXE HERO’S HANDBOOK DELUXE HERO’S HANDBOOK As heroes earn power points through adventuring and spend them to improve their traits, they will eventually run into the limits imposed by the series power level (see Power Level in Chapter 1).
modifiers.raw: GAMEMASTERING MUTANTS & MASTERMINDS MUTANTS & MASTERMINDS INCREASING POWER LEVEL WEALTH DELUXE HERO’S HANDBOOK DELUXE HERO’S HANDBOOK As heroes earn power points through adventuring and spend them to improve their traits, they will eventually run into the limits imposed by the series power level (see Power Level in Chapter 1).

modifiers.description: For a while, this can be a good thing, since the power level limits encourage play- ers to diversify their heroes:
modifiers.raw: For a while, this can be a good thing, since the power level limits encourage play- ers to diversify their heroes:

modifiers.description: However, sooner or later, you’re going to want to raise the series power level, giving heroes a bit more room for advancement and spending their earned power points.
modifiers.raw: However, sooner or later, you’re going to want to raise the series power level, giving heroes a bit more room for advancement and spending their earned power points.

modifiers.description: A good guideline is to follow the starting power point to- tals when it comes to power level:
modifiers.raw: A good guideline is to follow the starting power point to- tals when it comes to power level:

modifiers.description: When the heroes ac- cumulate an additional 15 power points from the start of the series or the last time the power level was raised, it’s probably time to consider raising the power level by one.
modifiers.raw: When the heroes ac- cumulate an additional 15 power points from the start of the series or the last time the power level was raised, it’s probably time to consider raising the power level by one.

modifiers.description: So a power level 10 series starts out with 150-point heroes.
modifiers.raw: So a power level 10 series starts out with 150-point heroes.

modifiers.description: When they have earned another 15 power points (bringing their total up to 165), the GM should consider raising the series power level to 11, allowing the heroes to spend some of those points to increase traits currently at the maximum limit.
modifiers.raw: When they have earned another 15 power points (bringing their total up to 165), the GM should consider raising the series power level to 11, allowing the heroes to spend some of those points to increase traits currently at the maximum limit.

modifiers.description: When you increase the series power level, you should also re-evaluate the capabilities of the villains and other challenges the heroes face.
modifiers.raw: When you increase the series power level, you should also re-evaluate the capabilities of the villains and other challenges the heroes face.

modifiers.description: While NPCs don’t “earn” power points, and aren’t even subject to the same power level limits as the heroes, you should feel free to improve the traits of non-player characters in the series to keep pace with the heroes, ensuring those antagonists remain a suit- able challenge.
modifiers.raw: While NPCs don’t “earn” power points, and aren’t even subject to the same power level limits as the heroes, you should feel free to improve the traits of non-player characters in the series to keep pace with the heroes, ensuring those antagonists remain a suit- able challenge.

modifiers.description: It’s also fine to have others lag behind, as the heroes outstrip some of their old foes, who no longer represent the kind of threat they did before, plus you can always introduce new villains and challenges suited to the series power level as things progress.
modifiers.raw: It’s also fine to have others lag behind, as the heroes outstrip some of their old foes, who no longer represent the kind of threat they did before, plus you can always introduce new villains and challenges suited to the series power level as things progress.

modifiers.description: As the series progresses, you may want to slow the rate of increasing power level, stretching it out to even 20, 30, or more earned power points.
modifiers.raw: As the series progresses, you may want to slow the rate of increasing power level, stretching it out to even 20, 30, or more earned power points.

modifiers.description: They’re good examples of how heroes can develop in a series where the power level has become “fixed” but the GM still provides power point awards for advancement.
modifiers.raw: They’re good examples of how heroes can develop in a series where the power level has become “fixed” but the GM still provides power point awards for advancement.

modifiers.description: A fun option can be to use your home city (or one near- by) as the setting for your series.
modifiers.raw: A fun option can be to use your home city (or one near- by) as the setting for your series.

modifiers.description: These characters are intended to be used when the GM needs a fairly common type of character that’s either around to help or harm the character in some way depending on your series.
modifiers.raw: These characters are intended to be used when the GM needs a fairly common type of character that’s either around to help or harm the character in some way depending on your series.

modifiers.description: They’re also meant to represent a wide range of characters of that type.
modifiers.raw: They’re also meant to represent a wide range of characters of that type.

modifiers.description: If you don’t see exactly the archetype you need, find something close and make a few changes.
modifiers.raw: If you don’t see exactly the archetype you need, find something close and make a few changes.

modifiers.description: The reporter archetype can also be used for any other type of professional by swapping out the Expertise specialties for others.
modifiers.raw: The reporter archetype can also be used for any other type of professional by swapping out the Expertise specialties for others.

modifiers.description: Abilities 22 + Powers 0 + Advantages 3 + Skills 17 + Defenses 5 = 47 This archetype focuses primarily on uniformed beat cops.
modifiers.raw: Abilities 22 + Powers 0 + Advantages 3 + Skills 17 + Defenses 5 = 47 This archetype focuses primarily on uniformed beat cops.

modifiers.description: Abilities 16 + Powers 0 + Advantages 3 + Skills 16 + Defenses 10 = 45 This archetype represents any sort of militant from militia members to terrorists—anyone who’s trained and ready to kill or die for their cause.
modifiers.raw: Abilities 16 + Powers 0 + Advantages 3 + Skills 16 + Defenses 10 = 45 This archetype represents any sort of militant from militia members to terrorists—anyone who’s trained and ready to kill or die for their cause.

modifiers.description: Abilities 20 + Powers 0 + Advantages 11 + Skills 9 + Defenses 8 = 48 This archetype covers the typical infantryman or enlisted soldier.
modifiers.raw: Abilities 20 + Powers 0 + Advantages 11 + Skills 9 + Defenses 8 = 48 This archetype covers the typical infantryman or enlisted soldier.

modifiers.description: Abilities 14 + Powers 0 + Advantages 2 + Skills 16 + Defenses 5 = 37 This archetype represents run-of-the-mill career crimi- nals.
modifiers.raw: Abilities 14 + Powers 0 + Advantages 2 + Skills 16 + Defenses 5 = 37 This archetype represents run-of-the-mill career crimi- nals.

modifiers.description: For an even more influential gang leader, use the crime lord archetype.
modifiers.raw: For an even more influential gang leader, use the crime lord archetype.

modifiers.description: Players can spend their heroes’ awarded power points in-between adventures to improve the heroes’ traits, limited only by the series power level.
modifiers.raw: Players can spend their heroes’ awarded power points in-between adventures to improve the heroes’ traits, limited only by the series power level.

modifiers.description: They can also choose to save up unspent power points, waiting until the power level increases, in order to spend them to improve a trait already at its maximum rating or rank.
modifiers.raw: They can also choose to save up unspent power points, waiting until the power level increases, in order to spend them to improve a trait already at its maximum rating or rank.

modifiers.description: INCREASING POWER LEVEL As heroes earn power points through adventuring and spend them to improve their traits, they will eventually run into the limits imposed by the series power level (see Power Level, earlier in this chapter, for details).
modifiers.raw: INCREASING POWER LEVEL As heroes earn power points through adventuring and spend them to improve their traits, they will eventually run into the limits imposed by the series power level (see Power Level, earlier in this chapter, for details).

modifiers.description: For a while, this can be a good thing, since the power level limits encourage heroes to diversify and acquire new skills, ad- vantages, and powers rather than simply pumping points into their existing traits to increase them to unwieldy lev- els.
modifiers.raw: For a while, this can be a good thing, since the power level limits encourage heroes to diversify and acquire new skills, ad- vantages, and powers rather than simply pumping points into their existing traits to increase them to unwieldy lev- els.

modifiers.description: However, sooner or later, you’re going to want to raise the power level, giving the heroes a bit more breathing room for advancement and spending their earned power points.
modifiers.raw: However, sooner or later, you’re going to want to raise the power level, giving the heroes a bit more breathing room for advancement and spending their earned power points.

modifiers.description: A good guideline is to follow the starting power point to- tals when it comes to power level:
modifiers.raw: A good guideline is to follow the starting power point to- tals when it comes to power level:

modifiers.description: when the heroes ac- cumulate an additional 15 power points from the start of the series or the last time the power level was raised, it’s probably time to raise the power level by +1.
modifiers.raw: when the heroes ac- cumulate an additional 15 power points from the start of the series or the last time the power level was raised, it’s probably time to raise the power level by +1.

modifiers.description: So a power level 10 game starts out with 150-point heroes.
modifiers.raw: So a power level 10 game starts out with 150-point heroes.

modifiers.description: When they have earned another 15 power points (bringing their to- tal up to 165), the GM should consider raising the power level to 11, allowing the heroes to spend some of those power points to increase traits which are currently at the maximum limit.
modifiers.raw: When they have earned another 15 power points (bringing their to- tal up to 165), the GM should consider raising the power level to 11, allowing the heroes to spend some of those power points to increase traits which are currently at the maximum limit.

modifiers.description: When you increase the power level, you should also re- evaluate the capabilities of the villains and other chal- lenges the heroes face.
modifiers.raw: When you increase the power level, you should also re- evaluate the capabilities of the villains and other chal- lenges the heroes face.

modifiers.description: While NPCs don’t earn additional power points as such, and aren’t even subject to the same power level limits as the heroes, you should feel free to improve the traits of some non-player characters to keep pace with the heroes, ensuring those antagonists remain a suitable challenge.
modifiers.raw: While NPCs don’t earn additional power points as such, and aren’t even subject to the same power level limits as the heroes, you should feel free to improve the traits of some non-player characters to keep pace with the heroes, ensuring those antagonists remain a suitable challenge.

modifiers.description: It’s also fine to have others lag be- hind, as the heroes outstrip some of their old foes, who no longer represent the kind of threat they did before, plus you can introduce new villains and challenges suited to the series power level as things progress.
modifiers.raw: It’s also fine to have others lag be- hind, as the heroes outstrip some of their old foes, who no longer represent the kind of threat they did before, plus you can introduce new villains and challenges suited to the series power level as things progress.
